Burning odours, sparking, partial power with safety risk, water in boards or total loss affecting vulnerable occupants typically qualify.
Will the engineer complete full repairs at 2am?
Make-safe is the priority. Permanent repairs proceed when parts, access and safe working conditions allow—sometimes the next day.
Are emergency rates different from daytime rates?

4k-cctv-system.name: 4K CCTV System
4k-cctv-system.intro: A 4K CCTV system captures ultra-high-definition video for wide scenes where you still need to digitally zoom for faces, clothing or vehicle detail. Icomply design and install 4K camera systems across the North West with NVRs, switches and hard drives sized for the higher bandwidth and storage that true 4K demands.
4k-cctv-system.body: 4K is not automatically better if lenses, lighting or network are weak — we match 4K cameras to views that benefit from the resolution, and use HD elsewhere to control cost. Designs include PoE budgets, uplink capacity, retention days at 4K bitrates and client PC/mobile playback performance. Ideal for large retail floors, warehouses, car-park overviews and main entrances. Stockport engineers commission focusing, compression settings and remote access for commercial clients across Greater Manchester.

Hard-drive retention planning at 4K settings
Commissioning for focus, compression and remote view
faq: Do I need 4K on every camera?
Usually no. Use 4K on wide or critical views and HD on simpler coverage to balance cost, storage and network load.
Will 4K work on my existing recorder?
Only if the recorder and licenses support 4K streams and your network can carry them. Many upgrades need a new NVR and drives.
Does 4K help at night?
Night quality depends on sensor, IR/illumination and lens as much as megapixels. We specify low-light capable 4K models where night detail matters.

faq: How long do AOV actuators last?
Lifespan varies with duty cycle, weather exposure and maintenance — seized vents and weak batteries shorten motor life dramatically.

Actuators are life-safety components on often difficult access routes; professional fitting and re-commissioning is strongly recommended.

An automatic opening vent system opens smoke vents or windows when fire is detected, helping keep escape routes clearer of smoke — common in flats and multi-storey buildings.

Usually yes — AOVs should open on relevant fire signals and often include manual override and cause-and-effect testing during commissioning.

Small single-vent jobs may complete in a day; multi-core residential schemes take longer and are programmed around access and weather for roof works.

Cabling, power and termination quality make the difference between crisp speech and constant crackle; our engineers test every leg before handover. faq: When is audio intercom better than video?
When budget, simplicity or industrial noise control is the priority and visual ID is already covered by staff presence or CCTV.
Can audio intercoms open doors?

How do you improve speech on noisy sites?
Station type, placement, gain settings and acoustic hoods all help. We survey ambient noise rather than guessing.

automatic-emergency-lighting-test.name: Automatic Emergency Lighting Test
automatic-emergency-lighting-test.intro: Automatic emergency lighting test systems — self-test luminaires, addressable test networks and DALI-style monitoring — cut the labour of manual monthly checks while still meeting BS 5266 expectations. Icomply specify, install and commission automatic test solutions for offices, care premises, retail and multi-let buildings across Greater Manchester and the North West, with handover that shows how reports map to your logbook.
automatic-emergency-lighting-test.body: Self-testing fittings and automatic test panels flag faults early, but they still need competent review of results and periodic full-duration verification. Our engineers set up test schedules, train site staff on reading status LEDs or software dashboards, and integrate automatic testing into planned maintenance contracts. Based in Stockport, we upgrade legacy manual estates, retrofit self-test LED conversions and investigate false failures so compliance stays practical rather than paper-only. Fixed-price quotes cover survey, install, programming and certification paperwork.
automatic-emergency-lighting-test.meta_desc: Automatic emergency lighting test systems install & setup. Self-test, BS 5266 records. Stockport, Manchester & North West — Icomply.

Integration with planned emergency lighting contracts
faq: Does automatic testing replace all emergency lighting visits?
No. Automatic tests reduce routine manual switching, but you still need competent oversight of results and periodic full-duration or service visits as required by BS 5266 and the manufacturer.
Can you retrofit automatic testing to existing fittings?
Often yes via self-test LED conversions or addressable modules, subject to wiring and compatibility — we survey first and quote fixed scope.
Will automatic test records satisfy auditors?
When results are reviewed, failures remedied and duration tests recorded correctly, automatic systems support a robust BS 5266 logbook.

automatic-opening-vent.name: Automatic Opening Vent
automatic-opening-vent.intro: An automatic opening vent (AOV) is a smoke vent or window that opens on fire detection or manual control to help clear smoke from escape routes. Icomply supply, install and maintain automatic opening vents — roof hatches, casement windows and louvre banks — for residential and commercial properties across Greater Manchester and the North West, with full electrical control and certification.
automatic-opening-vent.body: Correct free area, weather performance and reliable actuation matter as much as the vent itself. We match vent type to the architectural opening, fit actuators with appropriate force and stroke, connect to the AOV panel and prove operation under backup power. Retrofit projects on occupied blocks are planned carefully for access equipment and resident communication. After install, planned maintenance keeps hinges, seals and motors serviceable through seasonal weather.
automatic-opening-vent.meta_desc: Automatic opening vent supply, install & service. Roof, window & louvre AOVs. Stockport, Greater Manchester & North West.
focus_points: Specification of roof, window and louvre automatic vents
Actuator sizing, fitting and weather-seal integrity
Panel connection and backup-power proving
Aftercare maintenance for reliable long-term operation
faq: Are automatic opening vents the same as smoke vents?
AOVs are a common form of automatic smoke vent used in escape-route smoke control; exact product choice depends on design free area and location.
Can an existing window become an AOV?
Sometimes, if the frame, free area and actuator mounting are suitable — we assess case by case rather than forcing a retrofit.
Who can operate the vent manually?
Manual break-glass or key switches are usually provided for firefighters and trained staff, tested during commissioning and service.
automatic-opening-vent.seo_keywords: automatic opening vent, AOV vent, automatic smoke vent, automatic opening window vent, AOV Manchester, automatic opening vent Stockport, smoke vent install North West, BS 9991 vent

automatic-opening-vent-installation.name: Automatic Opening Vent Installation
automatic-opening-vent-installation.intro: Need automatic opening vent installation that meets smoke-control and life-safety expectations? Icomply design, supply and install AOV systems for apartment blocks, commercial buildings and mixed-use developments across Greater Manchester and the North West. Working to BS 9991 and EN 12101 practice, we fit roof vents, façade vents and actuators linked to smoke control panels so escape routes stay tenable when it matters.
automatic-opening-vent-installation.body: Automatic opening vents are a core part of many natural smoke ventilation strategies. Our engineers survey shaft, stair and corridor arrangements, confirm vent sizes and free area requirements against your fire strategy, and install actuators, vents, weather controls and interfaces to the AOV panel and fire alarm cause-and-effect. Commissioning includes open/close timing, battery standby checks, manual override tests and certification-ready records. We also retrofit AOVs on existing blocks where older vents have failed or never performed correctly. Based in Stockport, Icomply supports landlords, developers, main contractors and FM teams with fixed-price quotes after survey and ongoing AOV service contracts.

Supply and installation of AOV vents, actuators and weather controls
Panel interfacing, cause-and-effect testing and commissioning records
Retrofit upgrades and planned AOV maintenance after install
faq: Do AOVs need to link to the fire alarm?
Most life-safety AOVs operate from the smoke control panel with fire alarm or detection inputs defined in the cause-and-effect. We implement the strategy signed off for your building.
Can you install AOVs on occupied residential blocks?
Yes. We plan access, weather protection and temporary measures carefully, often working roof zones in controlled phases.

Installations are approached under BS 9991 smoke-control practice and EN 12101 product/system expectations, with documentation for building management and insurers.

automatic-smoke-vent.name: Automatic Smoke Vent
automatic-smoke-vent.intro: An automatic smoke vent opens without manual intervention when fire is detected, releasing smoke from protected routes. Icomply supply, install and service automatic smoke vents for residential and commercial buildings across Greater Manchester, Cheshire, Lancashire and Merseyside — integrating actuators, panels and detection interfaces for dependable EN 12101-aligned performance.
automatic-smoke-vent.body: We select vent types suited to roofs, façades and shafts, install weatherproofing carefully and commission automatic operation alongside manual firefighter controls. Service contracts catch corrosion, motor wear and battery ageing before a real fire event. If a vent fails open or closed, reactive repair restores the correct fail-safe behaviour. Fixed-price quotes cover new vents, replacements and multi-vent cores after a short survey.
automatic-smoke-vent.meta_desc: Automatic smoke vent install, repair & service. Roof & façade AOVs with panel control. Stockport, Manchester & North West.

Actuator and control integration
Commissioning of automatic and manual operation
Repair and planned service for long-term reliability
faq: How does an automatic smoke vent know when to open?
Usually via a signal from the fire alarm or dedicated smoke detection linked to the AOV control panel, plus manual override points.

Standards and RCD expectations have tightened. An inspection applies current safety thinking to what is still in service.

beam-detection.name: Beam Detection
beam-detection.intro: Beam detection protects wide open spaces by projecting an infrared beam across the ceiling void or high roof space. Icomply installs and aligns reflective and end-to-end beam detectors for North West warehouses, atria and sports halls.
beam-detection.body: Correct mounting height, separation and alignment are critical; we use manufacturer tools to set thresholds that catch real smoke without constant misalignment faults. Building movement and temperature swings are considered at design stage. Service visits re-check alignment and lens cleanliness so coverage stays reliable.
beam-detection.meta_desc: Beam smoke detection for warehouses & atria across Stockport, Manchester & the North West. Align, commission & service with Icomply.
focus_points: Reflective and transmitter/receiver beam detector options
Survey for mounting height, range and structural movement
Precision alignment and threshold commissioning
Maintenance to prevent dirt and drift-related faults
faq: When is beam detection recommended?
Large open floor plates with high ceilings where point detectors are impractical or insufficient often suit beam detection.
Will forklift exhaust set beams off?
Good design, correct sensitivity and sometimes multi-criteria strategies reduce nuisance alarms; we assess process risks on survey.
Can beams work with an addressable fire panel?
Yes. Beams are interfaced as monitored inputs or addressable modules depending on the chosen products.
beam-detection.seo_keywords: beam detection, beam smoke detector Stockport, warehouse beam detection North West, aspirating vs beam Manchester, fire beam install, Icomply

beam-detection-system.name: Beam Detection System
beam-detection-system.intro: A beam detection system uses projected optical beams to monitor large open volumes—warehouses, atria, sports halls and factories—where point detectors would be impractical. Icomply design, install and align reflective and end-to-end beam detectors to BS 5839 practice across the North West. From Stockport we survey mounting heights, building movement and sunlight paths so beams stay stable and false alarms stay low.
beam-detection-system.body: Correct alignment, bracket rigidity and contamination planning are everything with beams. We install, commission with obscuration tests, integrate to the fire panel and train staff on temporary isolation during dust-producing works. Service visits re-check alignment and clean optics so warehouse changes do not silently defeat detection.
beam-detection-system.meta_desc: Beam detection system install & alignment for warehouses and atria. BS 5839 — North West. Icomply Stockport.
focus_points: Open-area beam design and mounting survey
Precision alignment and commissioning tests
Fire panel integration and zoning
Optics clean and realignment on service visits
faq: Reflective or end-to-end beams?
Reflective units need power at one end and suit many warehouses; end-to-end can suit very long ranges. We select after measuring the space.
Will forklifts set beams off?
Poor siting can. We position beams above traffic where practical and set delay/sensitivity within manufacturer limits to reduce nuisance alarms.
Can beams work with high racking?
Racking alters smoke behaviour and line of sight. We may combine beams with in-rack or aspirating options as the risk requires.
beam-detection-system.seo_keywords: beam detection system, beam smoke detector installation, warehouse beam detection, optical beam fire detection, beam detection Manchester, beam detection Stockport, North West, Icomply

Scheduled testing under maintenance contracts

Yes as part of install kits or trade spares for homes that keep stock on site.
Can bedside calls be silent at night?
Night modes usually re-route indication rather than silence completely; we configure to your protocol.

Yes on many platforms — useful where cabling is impractical.

biometric-access-control.name: Biometric Access Control
biometric-access-control.intro: Biometric access control uses fingerprints or other biometric factors so credentials cannot be casually lent or copied like a shared fob. Icomply designs and installs biometric access control for North West sites that need stronger identity assurance—server rooms, labs, stock cages and selective office zones—always with privacy-aware enrolment processes and fallback credentials for edge cases. We balance security with practical throughput at busy doors.
biometric-access-control.body: Not every door needs biometrics; we help you apply them where risk justifies cost and user acceptance. Our Stockport engineers integrate biometric readers with controllers, schedule templates and audit logs, and train admins on enrolment and template management under UK data protection expectations. Outdoor or wet environments may need alternative technologies—we advise honestly. Fixed quotes cover hardware, programming and commissioning sheets. Combine with CCTV at high-security doors for richer incident evidence.
biometric-access-control.meta_desc: Biometric access control install across the North West. Fingerprint & multi-factor door security with privacy-aware setup. Icomply.
focus_points: Risk-based door selection for biometric vs card access
Reader install, controller integration and enrolment workflow
Fallback credentials and admin training for template management
Audit trails and commissioning documentation for FM teams
faq: Are fingerprints stored as images?
Reputable systems store mathematical templates, not full fingerprint pictures. We explain your chosen product’s storage model during design.
What if someone’s fingerprint will not enrol?
We configure alternative credentials (card/PIN) for a minority of users so nobody is locked out of their workplace.
Is biometric access suitable for apartments?
Sometimes for plant rooms or staff areas; resident main doors often suit cards or video door entry better. We recommend by use case.
biometric-access-control.seo_keywords: biometric access control, fingerprint door access, biometric reader install North West, biometric security Manchester, fingerprint entry Stockport, multi-factor door control

bs-4737.name: BS 4737
bs-4737.intro: BS 4737 is the historic British Standard many people still associate with intruder alarm systems in the UK. Modern installations more commonly reference PD 6662 and BS EN 50131, but understanding BS 4737 helps when maintaining or upgrading older systems. Icomply survey legacy BS 4737-era alarms across Greater Manchester and the North West, advise on compliant upgrade paths and install contemporary systems with clear documentation from our Stockport base.
bs-4737.body: If your insurance documents or building handbook still mention BS 4737, we translate that into practical recommendations for today’s standards and grades. Many older panels remain serviceable short-term but lack modern signalling, power supervision or parts availability. We can keep them working where sensible, or design a replacement that reuses cabling and improves detection. Facilities managers inherit mixed estates—we standardise reporting and phased upgrades so risk and cost stay controlled. Share any existing certificates or insurer clauses when requesting advice.
bs-4737.meta_desc: BS 4737 intruder alarms — survey, maintain or upgrade to modern PD 6662 / BS EN 50131 practice. North West engineers from Stockport.
focus_points: Assessment of legacy BS 4737-era alarm systems
Maintenance where still viable and safe
Upgrade design to current UK intruder standards practice
Documentation suitable for insurers and site records
faq: Is BS 4737 still the current standard for new alarms?
New professional systems are generally designed under PD 6662 using BS EN 50131. BS 4737 is largely historical but still referenced on older paperwork.
Can you maintain an old BS 4737 system?
Often yes, subject to spares. We will also flag when upgrade is the safer or more economical option.
Will upgrading help with insurance?
Modern graded systems with good documentation frequently better match current insurer expectations—confirm specific wording with your broker.
bs-4737.seo_keywords: BS 4737, BS 4737 alarm, PD 6662, BS EN 50131, upgrade old burglar alarm, intruder alarm standards UK, Manchester, Stockport

bs-5266.name: BS 5266
bs-5266.intro: BS 5266 is the key British Standard series guiding emergency lighting design, installation, testing and maintenance in the UK. Icomply apply BS 5266 principles on every emergency lighting project across Greater Manchester and the North West—from initial surveys to duration testing and certification. Based in Stockport, we translate the standard into practical site decisions for landlords, designers and facilities managers.
bs-5266.body: Working “to BS 5266” means more than quoting the number on a proposal. It influences lux levels on escape routes, open area coverage, high-risk task lighting, exit signage, duration, test facilities and record-keeping. Our engineers design and verify systems with those expectations in mind, and we explain trade-offs when existing buildings cannot achieve textbook layouts without structural change. Training for monthly user tests, annual competent testing and battery replacement cycles all form part of a BS 5266-minded service. If a consultant specification references BS 5266, we install and commission against it and document deviations only with client agreement.
bs-5266.meta_desc: BS 5266 emergency lighting design, install, test & maintain across GM & the North West. Practical compliance from Icomply Stockport. Fixed-price quotes.
focus_points: Design and installation guided by BS 5266 principles
Escape route, open area and high-risk task coverage
Testing regimes and documentation aligned to the standard
Remedial programmes for non-compliant existing systems
faq: Is BS 5266 a legal requirement?
Fire safety law requires suitable emergency lighting; BS 5266 is the widely accepted British Standard used to achieve and demonstrate that suitability in practice.
Does BS 5266 only apply to new buildings?
No. Existing premises are expected to maintain adequate emergency lighting; upgrades are often driven by FRAs, alterations or failed tests.
Can you review our system against BS 5266?
Yes. We survey, list non-conformities and quote prioritised remedials so you can plan spend against risk.
bs-5266.seo_keywords: BS 5266, BS 5266 emergency lighting, BS 5266 testing, BS 5266 installation, BS 5266 North West, BS 5266 Manchester, BS 5266 Stockport, emergency lighting British Standard

bs-5266-compliance-certificate.body: Based in Stockport (SK2), we cover Lancashire, Cheshire, Merseyside and Cumbria with same-week appointments where capacity allows. Certification follows competent testing—not a paper exercise—so we complete function and duration checks, record failures, complete agreed remedials, then retest before signing off. New installs receive commissioning documentation; existing systems receive service and duration evidence aligned to your logbook. Multi-site clients can standardise certificate formats across the portfolio. Related searches include emergency lighting surveys, battery replacement and full PPM contracts when certificates reveal wider estate issues.

faq: What does a BS 5266 compliance certificate show?
It evidences that emergency lighting has been inspected/tested against BS 5266 practice, including duration performance and noted defects or remedials.
Can you certificate a system you did not originally install?
Yes, after survey and testing we can service and certificate existing systems, flagging any design shortfalls that still need works.
How often should certification be renewed?
Follow BS 5266 test intervals—regular function tests and periodic full duration tests—plus re-certification after major changes or remedials.

bs-5266-emergency-lighting.name: BS 5266 Emergency Lighting
bs-5266-emergency-lighting.intro: BS 5266 emergency lighting sets the UK framework for escape route, open area and high-risk task lighting when mains power fails. Icomply design, install, test and certificate emergency lighting to BS 5266 for commercial, care and multi-occupied buildings across the North West. From Stockport our engineers deliver photometric-aware layouts, LED conversions, self-test options and the monthly/annual records responsible persons need for Fire Safety Order evidence.

bs-5839.name: BS 5839
bs-5839.intro: BS 5839 is the key British Standard series guiding design, installation, commissioning and maintenance of fire detection and fire alarm systems in non-domestic premises. Icomply applies BS 5839 practice on every relevant project from our Stockport base.
bs-5839.body: Clients often hear BS 5839 mentioned in risk assessments without a clear explanation of categories, grades or servicing intervals. bs-5839.meta_desc: BS 5839 fire alarm design, install, service & certification across Stockport, Greater Manchester & the North West. Expert support from Icomply.

Maintenance intervals and competent-person servicing
Documentation packs auditors recognise and accept
faq: What does BS 5839 cover?
It sets recommendations for fire detection and alarm systems in non-domestic buildings, including design categories, installation quality, commissioning and maintenance.
Is BS 5839 a legal requirement?
The law requires suitable fire safety measures; BS 5839 is the primary benchmark used to demonstrate that fire alarms meet that duty.
Which BS 5839 category do I need?
Categories such as L1–L5, M and P depend on risk and building use. We recommend a category after survey and review of your fire risk assessment.

Interfaces to AOV, door release and monitoring
Planned maintenance, logbooks and user training
faq: Which BS 5839 category do I need?
It depends on premises type and fire risk assessment—for example L1–L5 for automatic life protection variants or M for manual systems. We recommend a category after survey.
Do you upgrade non-compliant older systems?

bs-7671.name: BS 7671

bs-7671.body: Surge protection, AFDD consideration, RCD provisions and documentation rules evolve with each amendment; our engineers apply the edition relevant to your project. bs-7671.meta_desc: BS 7671 compliant electrical design, install and testing in the North West. Current Wiring Regulations practice.

Guidance when older installs pre-date new rules
faq: Does BS 7671 apply to commercial buildings?
Yes. It applies broadly to electrical installations in and around buildings, with additional standards sometimes relevant for specialist environments.
Must every existing installation be upgraded to the latest amendment?
Not automatically. New work must meet current requirements; existing installations are judged on safety and condition during inspection.
Where can I read BS 7671 myself?
The standard is published by the IET/BSI. We translate the clauses that affect your project into practical recommendations.

We inspect and install to the current edition and amendments in force at the time of the works or report.

Fixed installation inspection is separate from PAT of portable appliances—both can be arranged if you need a full electrical safety package.

Not automatically. Age is a factor, but condition, adequacy of protection and test results determine coding—not the calendar alone.

LD3 covers escape routes only; LD2 adds high-risk rooms. Local housing standards and your risk assessment determine which is appropriate.
Do HMOs need LD2?
Many larger or higher-risk HMOs require enhanced coverage. We check against licensing conditions and BS 5839-6 recommendations.
Are battery-only alarms acceptable?
Grade and power supply depend on property type and risk. We specify mains-powered with backup where the standard and licensing expect it.

Many commercial sites keep 14–31 days; higher-risk locations may need longer. We size drives to your policy and legal/insurance expectations.
Why has our CCTV stopped recording?
Typical causes include full or failed hard disks, power issues, wrong schedules or network faults on IP systems. We diagnose and quote repairs clearly.
Can you set up secure export of CCTV clips?
Yes. We configure authorised user accounts, show how to export standard formats, and can lock down guest access to protect privacy.
cctv-recording.seo_keywords: CCTV recording, NVR setup, DVR hard drive, CCTV storage North West, CCTV retention Manchester, recorder install Stockport, footage export, CCTV disk replacement

cctv-remote-viewing-setup.name: CCTV Remote Viewing Setup
cctv-remote-viewing-setup.intro: CCTV remote viewing setup lets owners and managers check live and recorded video from phones, tablets or PCs without being on site. Icomply configure secure remote access for new and existing CCTV systems across Greater Manchester, including router ports or P2P apps, strong credentials and multi-user permissions.
cctv-remote-viewing-setup.body: Many systems are installed without reliable remote access, or apps stop working after router changes. We audit your recorder, network and mobile devices, then implement a stable connection method suited to your router and IT policy. Training covers live view, playback, clip export and password changes. For multi-site clients we standardise app layouts so regional managers see consistent camera naming from Stockport to Liverpool and beyond.
cctv-remote-viewing-setup.meta_desc: CCTV remote viewing setup on phone & PC. Secure app configuration across Greater Manchester, Stockport & the North West.
focus_points: Secure remote access configuration for recorders
Smartphone and desktop app pairing
User permissions and password hygiene guidance
Multi-site naming and layout standardisation
faq: Why did my CCTV app stop working?
Common causes include router swaps, changed Wi-Fi passwords, expired P2P services, firmware issues or ISP CGNAT. We diagnose and restore a working method where the hardware allows.
Is remote viewing safe?
It is when strong unique passwords, firmware updates and sensible network practices are used. We avoid weak default credentials and explain basic hardening during setup.
Can multiple people view the same system?
Yes. We create separate logins where the platform allows so access can be revoked when staff leave.
cctv-remote-viewing-setup.seo_keywords: CCTV remote viewing setup, CCTV phone app setup, remote CCTV access, CCTV live view configuration, remote viewing North West, CCTV app Manchester, CCTV remote Stockport

cctv-repair.name: CCTV Repair
cctv-repair.intro: Blurry images, dead cameras or a recorder that will not play back? Icomply provide CCTV repair across the North West for homes, shops, offices and industrial sites. Our Stockport engineers diagnose camera, cable, PoE, switch and NVR/DVR faults, restore recording where possible and advise honestly when replacement is better value than repair.
cctv-repair.body: Typical repairs include failed IR arrays, water-ingress cameras, crushed cables, overloaded PoE switches, corrupt storage and misconfigured remote apps. We re-establish viewing for managers and owners, re-aim cameras after storms or vandalism, and document changes for site security folders. Commercial clients can request priority attendance when coverage of cash or loading areas is lost. Share recorder brand, camera count and fault symptoms when booking so we bring likely spares.
cctv-repair.meta_desc: CCTV repair — cameras, cables & NVR/DVR faults fixed. Engineers from Stockport across Greater Manchester & the North West.

On-site repair or clear replace recommendations
Restore local and remote viewing after fix
Commercial priority options subject to capacity
faq: Can you recover footage from a failed recorder?
Sometimes drives can be imaged or replaced with data retained, but success is not guaranteed. We assess options on site and prioritise restoring live recording.
Do you repair both analogue and IP CCTV?
Yes. We work on HD-over-coax and IP systems, including hybrid recorders common on upgraded sites.
Is night vision failure repairable?
IR board or camera replacement often restores night performance; we quote the economical option after inspection.

cctv-servicing.intro: CCTV servicing is a structured health check of your entire video system—not just a wipe of the lenses. Icomply deliver professional CCTV servicing for residential and commercial clients across Greater Manchester and the wider North West, verifying image quality, recording integrity, timestamps and user access. From Stockport we schedule services that fit trading hours and provide written reports for facilities and compliance files.
cctv-servicing.body: During a service we review camera positions against current site risks, check privacy masking where GDPR/DPA siting matters, confirm retention settings and test playback from the recorder. After servicing you know whether the system still meets the purpose it was installed for, or whether extra cameras or storage are advisable. Bundle servicing with intruder alarm visits to reduce engineer days on site.

Written service report with upgrade recommendations
faq: Is CCTV servicing the same as cleaning cameras?
Cleaning is only one element. Servicing also checks recording, storage, power, networking and whether coverage still matches site risks.
How long does CCTV servicing take?
Small systems may take one to two hours; larger multi-camera commercial sites can take half a day or more.
Will you update remote viewing apps during service?

Can you design to an insurer’s specification?
Yes. Share the requirements and we map cameras, recording and monitoring notes against them where practical.

Proposals issued to you remain a record of recommended scope. Hardware prices may need refresh if a long delay occurs.

Written findings with fixed-price remedial options
faq: How often should CCTV systems be tested?
Many commercial sites benefit from at least annual testing, with interim checks after storms, power events or major IT changes. Higher-risk sites may test more often.
What do you need on site for a CCTV test?
Access to the recorder, admin credentials (or a named contact), and entry to camera locations. Roof or high-level access equipment is arranged if required.
Will testing disrupt live recording?
We work carefully to avoid wiping footage. Brief maintenance windows may be needed for reboots or disk work and are agreed in advance.

Not always. Quality existing cable can often be reused; we test first and only rewire weak or underspecified runs.

central-battery-emergency-lighting.name: Central Battery Emergency Lighting
central-battery-emergency-lighting.intro: Central battery emergency lighting feeds multiple luminaires from a shared battery system—useful on larger or specialised sites where central maintenance is preferred. Icomply design, install, service and test central battery emergency lighting across the North West in line with BS 5266 practice. From Stockport our engineers handle battery rooms, distribution, slave luminaires and duration testing with documentation for estates and compliance teams.
central-battery-emergency-lighting.body: Central systems need correct cable sizing, sub-circuit protection, ventilation for battery areas and disciplined isolation procedures. We survey whether a central battery approach truly fits versus modern self-contained LED—sometimes a hybrid is best. Maintenance includes battery condition, chargers, distribution boards and luminaire health, with full system duration tests planned to manage risk during discharge. When batteries reach end of life we quote replacement with minimal downtime strategies. If your site already has a central system showing faults or incomplete drawings, we can reverse-engineer, label circuits and restore a maintainable state.
central-battery-emergency-lighting.meta_desc: Central battery emergency lighting design, install & service across GM & the North West. BS 5266 testing from Icomply Stockport. Survey & fixed quotes.
focus_points: Central battery system design and installation
Charger, battery bank and distribution maintenance
Slave luminaire repairs and circuit labelling
Planned duration testing with risk-managed discharge windows
faq: Is central battery better than self-contained?
It depends on building scale, access for local battery changes and client maintenance preference. We compare whole-life cost and resilience before recommending.
How disruptive is a central battery duration test?
The whole system discharges together, so planning and temporary procedures matter. We schedule carefully with the responsible person.
Can you maintain older central battery systems?
Yes where safe and parts remain available; otherwise we propose staged migration to modern solutions.
central-battery-emergency-lighting.seo_keywords: central battery emergency lighting, central battery system, CBS emergency lighting, central battery emergency lighting North West, central battery Manchester, central battery Stockport, BS 5266 central battery

Advice when upgrades beat repeated repairs
faq: What does certified mean in practice?
It means the electrician is assessed under a recognised competence scheme and works to current Wiring Regulations, issuing statutory-style paperwork where required.
Will I always get a certificate?
New circuits, rewires and many alterations need formal certificates. Purely diagnostic visits may produce a report instead if no installation work is carried out.
Can a certified electrician sign off someone else’s work?
Signing off requires adequate inspection and testing of that work. We only certify installations we have properly verified.

circuit-breaker-repair.name: Circuit Breaker Repair
circuit-breaker-repair.intro: Circuit breaker repair and replacement restores protection when devices fail to reset, overheat or no longer match circuit design. iComply assesses whether a breaker can be replaced like-for-like or whether board limitations force a wider panel upgrade.
circuit-breaker-repair.body: Incorrect ratings and mixed manufacturer stacks create hidden risks; we check cable size, load and discrimination before fitting new devices. RCBO conversions are common where individual circuit protection is preferred. Do not keep resetting a breaker that trips immediately—call for diagnosis first.
circuit-breaker-repair.meta_desc: Circuit breaker repair and RCBO replacement across the North West. Safe ratings, testing and board advice.
focus_points: MCB, RCBO and RCD assessment
Correct rating and type selection
Overheating and nuisance trip investigation
Board compatibility checks before parts order
faq: Is a breaker that feels hot always faulty?
Heat can mean overload, loose terminations or a failing device. Measurement and inspection identify which applies.
Can I upgrade one breaker without changing the whole board?
Often yes if the board accepts the device type. Some older boards have limited or obsolete spares, which we flag early.
What is the difference between MCB and RCBO?
An MCB provides overcurrent protection; an RCBO adds residual current protection on the same device for that circuit.
circuit-breaker-repair.seo_keywords: circuit breaker repair, MCB replacement, RCBO install, breaker trips electrician, circuit breaker North West

Most systems continue local door decisions for stored credentials; remote changes wait until connectivity returns. We explain each platform’s offline mode at survey.
Is cloud access control secure enough for offices?

Can we manage multiple buildings in one cloud account?
Yes — multi-site management is a primary reason organisations choose cloud access control.

commercial-security-lighting.name: Commercial Security Lighting
commercial-security-lighting.intro: Planning commercial security lighting for offices, warehouses, retail parks or industrial estates? Icomply deliver commercial-grade LED security lighting schemes that improve staff safety, deter trespass and support CCTV evidence quality across Greater Manchester and the North West. Engineers survey risk areas, specify switching and lux levels, install to a fixed price after agreement, and leave clear controls guidance for facilities teams.
commercial-security-lighting.body: Tell us your postcode, site type and any existing column or façade infrastructure — we aim to reply within 2 hours on business days. Commercial projects often need zoned lighting: dusk-to-dawn for main yards, PIR boost for side alleys, and manual override for loading operations. We use durable IP-rated luminaires, appropriate circuit protection and neat containment suitable for plant areas and public-facing elevations. Stockport-based teams can programme multi-building estates under one purchase order, and coordinate with emergency lighting upgrades so escape routes and security zones are handled together where useful. Ideal for landlords, FM providers and owner-occupiers seeking one accountable contractor.
commercial-security-lighting.meta_desc: Commercial security lighting across Greater Manchester & the North West. LED schemes for yards, façades & estates — Icomply fixed quotes.
focus_points: Commercial site survey, zoning plan and fixed-price lighting quote
LED security and operational lighting for yards, docks and façades
Zoned photocell, timer and PIR control with FM-friendly overrides
Multi-building programmes and optional CCTV coordination
faq: Can commercial security lighting reduce energy costs?
Modern LED fittings with photocells and PIRs typically cut running costs versus old SON or halogen floods while improving light quality.
Do you work out of hours on trading sites?
Yes. We can schedule install and testing outside peak trading hours where site access and safety allow.
Is design included in the quote?
Survey-based specification and aiming guidance are included so you receive a clear fixed-price scope before works begin.

communal-door-entry-system.name: Communal Door Entry System
communal-door-entry-system.intro: A communal door entry system is essential for apartment blocks, sheltered housing and shared porches where one street door serves many households. Icomply designs, installs and maintains communal door entry systems across the North West so residents can identify callers, release the door safely and keep unauthorised access down. We specify robust outdoor panels, multi-way indoor handsets or video monitors, reliable lock interfaces and clear directories or button layouts that match the building’s flat numbering.
communal-door-entry-system.body: Projects include new communal entrances, replacement of failing shared systems and upgrades that add video or better vandal resistance. We work with freeholders, RMCs, managing agents and housing providers from Stockport across Greater Manchester, Cheshire, Lancashire and Merseyside. Commissioning includes per-flat call tests, release timing checks and simple resident guidance. Pair communal door entry with controlled fob access when your fire risk assessment and tenancy rules require both visitor call and resident credentials.
communal-door-entry-system.meta_desc: Communal door entry systems for apartment blocks across the North West. Icomply install, upgrade & maintain multi-flat audio/video entry. Fixed quotes after survey.
focus_points: Multi-flat communal panel and handset design
Vandal-resistant outdoor stations and reliable lock release
Per-flat call testing and clear directory or button mapping
Works for RMCs, agents, freeholders and housing providers
faq: Who pays for communal door entry repairs?
Usually the freeholder or managing agent via service charge, depending on leases. We invoice the instructing party and provide reports suitable for board or RMC records.
Can one panel serve a large block?
Yes, with correct capacity and directory type. Very large sites may use multiple panels or networked IP door entry — we size this on survey.
What if some handsets work and others do not?
That pattern often points to individual handsets or lateral cables. We fault-find systematically so you do not replace a whole system unnecessarily.

consumer-unit-upgrade.name: Consumer Unit Upgrade
consumer-unit-upgrade.intro: Upgrading a consumer unit is one of the most effective ways to bring an older electrical installation closer to modern BS 7671 expectations—especially where residual current protection is missing or the board is overloaded. Icomply plans and installs consumer unit upgrades for homes, landlords and light commercial premises across Stockport, Greater Manchester and the North West. We assess existing circuits, surge and RCD/RCBO strategy, then swap boards with minimal disruption and full certification on completion.
consumer-unit-upgrade.body: A good upgrade is more than a metal box change: circuit labelling, earthing and bonding checks, and correct protective device selection all matter. Where cables or circuits are not fit for a new board, we explain options before work starts so you are not surprised mid-job. Metal dual-RCD or all-RCBO boards are specified to suit the property and your budget. After energising we test, issue an Electrical Installation Certificate for the work, and leave a tidy cupboard you can understand at a glance.
consumer-unit-upgrade.meta_desc: Consumer unit upgrade to BS 7671 across Greater Manchester & the North West. RCD/RCBO boards, testing & certificates from Icomply Stockport.
focus_points: Survey of circuits, earthing and board location
RCD or all-RCBO consumer unit options to BS 7671
Labelling, testing and Electrical Installation Certificate
Landlord and homeowner upgrades with fixed agreed pricing
faq: How long does a consumer unit upgrade take?
Many domestic swaps complete in a day if circuits are healthy. Older or complex installs may need extra time—we confirm after survey.
Will I need a full rewire as well?
Not always. The upgrade can proceed if circuits are satisfactory; we only recommend rewires or partial rewires where testing shows genuine defects.
Do you notify Building Control?
Notifiable domestic work is handled under the appropriate Part P route by our qualified electricians, with certificates for your records and solicitor packs.

conventional-fire-alarm-panel.body: Zones are laid out so responding staff know which area needs attention, with sounder circuits and power supplies sized correctly. We avoid stretching conventional kits beyond their practical limits — recommending addressable upgrades when buildings grow complex. Handover includes zone charts that match the real floor plan.

faq: Are conventional fire alarms still compliant?
Yes, when correctly designed and maintained to BS 5839 for the building’s risk. Compliance depends on design quality, not only technology type.
How many zones do I need?
Enough to identify the fire location quickly without unnecessary cost. We set zone boundaries using floor plans and occupancy patterns.
Can a conventional panel monitor plant or lifts?
Ancillary relays can signal plant shutdowns and other interfaces; we include them in cause-and-effect design where required.

corridor-smoke-ventilation.name: Corridor Smoke Ventilation
corridor-smoke-ventilation.intro: Need corridor smoke ventilation that protects long residential or commercial escape routes? Icomply install and maintain corridor AOVs, shaft intakes/extracts and mechanical corridor smoke systems across the North West. Correct vent positions, free areas and control logic keep corridors clearer for longer—supporting evacuation and fire service access as set out in your fire strategy.
corridor-smoke-ventilation.body: Corridor smoke control may use automatic opening vents to shafts, mechanical extract, or combined arrangements with lobby ventilation. We implement the hardware: vents, actuators, dampers, fans and panels, then commission cause-and-effect including detection or fire alarm inputs. Service visits cycle devices, check batteries and list defects such as blocked shafts, painted-shut vents or failed actuators. Refurbishments often disturb corridor systems—we revalidate after builders leave. Landlords of large floorplates and double-loaded corridors rely on documented tests for audit and insurance. Stockport-based Icomply covers Greater Manchester and the wider region with fixed quotes and multi-site corridor smoke maintenance options.
corridor-smoke-ventilation.meta_desc: Corridor smoke ventilation install & service. AOV and mechanical solutions across Greater Manchester, Stockport & the North West.
focus_points: Corridor AOV and shaft ventilation installation to strategy
Mechanical corridor extract interfaces where designed
Functional testing, batteries and defect reporting
Post-refurbishment revalidation of corridor smoke systems
faq: Are corridor smoke vents the same as comfort windows?
No. Life-safety corridor vents are controlled as part of the smoke system with supervision and standby power—not everyday openable windows.
What if corridor vents have been painted shut?
We free or replace them as required, restore actuator travel, and retest. Decorators’ damage is a frequent finding on older blocks.
Do you coordinate with fire alarm engineers?
Yes. Cause-and-effect often needs joint testing; we work with your fire alarm provider or handle agreed interfaces as scoped.

cp12-gas-certificate.name: CP12 Gas Certificate
cp12-gas-certificate.intro: A CP12 gas certificate — the landlord gas safety record — is a legal requirement for most rented homes with gas appliances in the UK. Icomply Property Services arrange Gas Safe registered engineers to inspect and certificate landlord gas safety across Greater Manchester and the wider North West. From Stockport we support landlords, letting agents and portfolio managers with clear appointments, CP12/CP44 documentation where applicable, and straightforward remedial quotes if appliances fail checks.
cp12-gas-certificate.body: During a landlord gas safety visit, appliances and flues are examined for safe operation, visual condition and appropriate ventilation as required by Gas Safe practice. Passed appliances are recorded on the gas safety record issued to the landlord or agent for tenant files. Failed items are isolated or condemned following regulations, with clear next steps for repair or replacement. We help agents schedule annual cycles so certificates do not lapse across portfolios. Related works include carbon monoxide alarm installation and appliance servicing where requested. Coverage includes Manchester, Stockport, Cheshire, Lancashire, Merseyside and Cumbria with efficient multi-property booking for busy letting teams.
cp12-gas-certificate.meta_desc: CP12 gas certificate for landlords & agents. Gas Safe inspections across the North West. Icomply from Stockport.
focus_points: Landlord gas safety inspections by Gas Safe engineers
CP12 records issued for tenancy and agent files
Clear remedial advice if appliances fail safety checks
Portfolio scheduling for multi-property landlords and agents
faq: How often is a CP12 required?
Landlords must ensure gas safety checks are carried out at least every 12 months on relevant appliances and flues, and provide copies to tenants as required by law.
What if an appliance fails?
Unsafe appliances are dealt with under Gas Safe rules. We explain whether repair or replacement is needed and quote remediations clearly.
Do homeowners need a CP12?

disabled-toilet-alarm.name: Disabled Toilet Alarm
disabled-toilet-alarm.intro: Need a disabled toilet alarm fitted, repaired or periodically tested? Icomply install disabled toilet (accessible WC) alarm kits with pull cords, reset buttons, power supplies and over-door lights for commercial landlords, care homes and public buildings across the North West. Stockport engineers deliver neat installs and fixed-price multi-site programmes.
disabled-toilet-alarm.body: A disabled toilet alarm is a simple life-safety aid that is too often left broken. We supply and fit compliant-style kits, replace stretched cords, failed sounders and tired neon indicators with modern LEDs, and ensure the reset is inside the WC so staff must attend. For multi-storey buildings we label zones clearly at staff points. Integration with nurse call is available in care settings. PPM visits operate each alarm and record results for FM compliance folders. Reactive call-outs restore failed units quickly across Greater Manchester, Cheshire, Lancashire, Merseyside and Cumbria.

Cord, sounder and indicator repairs
Clear zoning for multi-toilet buildings
PPM testing with FM-ready records
faq: Where should the pull cord hang?
It should reach near floor level so a person who has fallen can still call — we set out to good practice and your risk assessment.
Why must the reset be inside the WC?
So assistance is confirmed in person rather than silenced remotely without attendance.
Can alarms link to a security desk?
Yes via relay or integrated systems — specified at survey.

dvr-installation.name: DVR Installation
dvr-installation.intro: DVR installation configures digital video recorders for coaxial and hybrid CCTV systems, setting recording schedules, motion detection, retention and user access. Icomply install and replace DVRs across the North West so older camera estates keep working reliably while you plan IP upgrades at your own pace.
dvr-installation.body: When a DVR fails or storage runs out, cameras may still be sound. We match replacement units to camera type (analogue, HD-over-coax or hybrid), install larger hard drives where needed, migrate settings and re-establish remote viewing. Rack mounting, UPS notes and ventilation are considered for plant cupboards and server rooms. Our Stockport engineers support shops, offices, landlords and industrial sites across Greater Manchester.
dvr-installation.meta_desc: DVR installation & replacement for CCTV systems. Hybrid recorders, storage & remote setup across Manchester & the North West.
focus_points: Compatible DVR / hybrid recorder specification
Hard-drive sizing for required retention
Recording schedule, motion and user setup
Remote viewing restoration after swap-out
faq: What is the difference between a DVR and an NVR?
DVRs typically record from coaxial analogue or HD-over-coax cameras; NVRs record from network IP cameras. Hybrid units can support mixed estates during upgrades.
Will a new DVR work with my old cameras?
Often yes if signal type matches. We test cameras on survey and advise if any need replacing for reliable HD recording.
Can you recover footage from a failed DVR?
Sometimes, depending on drive condition. We prioritise getting live recording restored; specialist data recovery may be needed for critical historic footage.
dvr-installation.seo_keywords: DVR installation, CCTV DVR install, DVR replacement, hybrid DVR CCTV, CCTV recorder installation North West, DVR setup Manchester, CCTV DVR Stockport

eicr.name: EICR
eicr.intro: An EICR (Electrical Installation Condition Report) is the formal inspection and testing process that grades the condition of fixed wiring against BS 7671. iComply delivers thorough EICRs for rental stock, workplaces and managed portfolios so you hold current evidence of electrical safety.
eicr.body: Qualified inspectors open distribution boards, sample circuits and record observations with clear C1, C2, C3 or FI coding. You receive a structured report, schedule of circuits and practical remedial advice rather than vague pass-fail language. Book a slot around tenant access windows and we coordinate attendance to minimise disruption.
eicr.meta_desc: EICR testing and Electrical Installation Condition Reports across Manchester and the North West. Clear coding and reports.
focus_points: Full fixed-wiring inspection to BS 7671
C1–C3 and FI observations explained in plain English
Circuit schedules and board labelling updates where needed
Remedial quotes aligned to report findings
faq: How often should an EICR be completed?
Private rented homes typically need a report at least every five years or on change of tenancy where required. Commercial intervals depend on risk, use and previous report recommendations.
What if the EICR is unsatisfactory?
Dangerous or potentially dangerous codes need prompt remedial work. We can price and complete repairs, then retest affected circuits so your paperwork reflects a safe installation.
Will tenants need to be present?
Access to all areas with electrical equipment is required. We schedule around occupancy and provide clear arrival windows to reduce missed appointments.
eicr.seo_keywords: EICR, Electrical Installation Condition Report, EICR certificate, EICR testing landlords, EICR North West Manchester

eicr-certificate.name: EICR Certificate
eicr-certificate.intro: Searching for an EICR certificate usually means you need formal proof that fixed electrics have been inspected and tested. Icomply issues Electrical Installation Condition Reports—widely referred to as EICR certificates—for private rentals, HMOs, offices and other premises across Greater Manchester and the North West. Often a misaligned strike, wrong voltage, failing magnet or continuous duty mismatch. We inspect mechanical and electrical causes together.

A strike releases the latch keep so a mechanical lock can open electrically; a maglock holds the door by electromagnet. Choice depends on door hardware and fire strategy.

escape-route-lighting.name: Escape Route Lighting
escape-route-lighting.intro: Escape route lighting keeps corridors, stairs and designated paths usable during evacuation when mains lighting is lost. Icomply design and deliver BS 5266 escape route lighting across Greater Manchester and the North West, balancing lux levels, glare control and robust fittings. From Stockport we help commercial and residential blocks close FRA actions that cite dark stairwells or uneven coverage.
escape-route-lighting.body: Escape routes need consistent light at floor level, on steps and near fire doors—not a single bulkhead at the far end of a corridor. We survey routes, measure practical performance and specify LED emergency luminaires with the correct maintained or non-maintained mode. Multi-storey stairs often need careful spacing to avoid dark landings. After install we commission and set a testing regime so batteries remain capable of full duration. For occupied buildings we work floor by floor, maintaining safe egress throughout the programme and coordinating with fire wardens where required.
escape-route-lighting.meta_desc: Escape route lighting to BS 5266 across GM & the North West. Corridor & stair emergency lighting from Icomply Stockport. Design, install & test.
focus_points: Design for corridors, stairs and changes of level
LED emergency luminaires spaced for usable floor illumination
Integration with exit signs and open area lighting
Testing regimes that keep route lighting reliable
faq: Is escape route lighting different from open area lighting?
Yes. Escape routes follow defined path requirements; open areas cover larger spaces where people may need to reach a route. Many buildings need both.
Our stairwell looks bright enough by day—do we still need emergency lights?
Daylight does not replace emergency lighting for night-time or internal routes without borrowed light. We assess actual conditions against BS 5266.
Can you improve escape route lighting without full rewire?
Often additional or replacement self-contained fittings can upgrade routes using existing electrical infrastructure after safety checks.
escape-route-lighting.seo_keywords: escape route lighting, emergency escape lighting, corridor emergency lighting, stair emergency lighting, escape route lighting North West, BS 5266 escape route, escape lighting Manchester, Stockport escape route lighting

Icomply design, supply and install maintained and non-maintained exit signage for offices, retail, blocks of flats, care premises and industrial units across Greater Manchester and the wider North West. We position signs so routes remain clear, integrate them with your emergency lighting circuit, and issue monthly/annual test records and duration certificates with clear handover notes.
exit-sign-installation.body: Tell us your postcode, property type and whether you need running-man pictograms, arrowed signs or dual-legend units — we aim to respond within 2 hours on business days. faq: Do exit signs need to be illuminated in the UK?

external-cctv-installation.intro: External CCTV installation protects façades, yards, fire exits and approach paths where most unauthorised access begins. Icomply fit weather-rated outdoor cameras across the North West with correct IP-rated housings, sealed cable entry, night illumination performance and mounts that survive wind and rain on commercial and domestic buildings.
external-cctv-installation.body: Outdoor design considers sunlight glare, IR reflection, mounting height against vandalism, and cable UV protection. We use bullets, turrets or outdoor domes as each elevation demands, and set wide overview plus tighter entrance views. Perimeter upgrades often combine external cameras with better entrance face capture. Installs include secure fixings into suitable substrates and testing after dark where night performance is critical. Stockport engineers cover Greater Manchester and neighbouring counties.

Entrance, yard and perimeter view design
faq: Will external cameras work in heavy rain and winter?
Quality IP-rated cameras are built for outdoor UK weather. We still seal connections and position units to reduce water and snow issues.
Do external cameras need special lighting?
Many include IR for mono night vision. Colour night or very long ranges may need supplementary lighting — assessed on survey.
Can you install cameras high on large buildings?
Yes with appropriate access equipment. Height access needs are priced into the quote for safety and compliance.

factory-fire-alarm-system.name: Factory Fire Alarm System
factory-fire-alarm-system.intro: Factory fire alarm systems face harsh environments: dust, heat, high ceilings, process plant and shift working. Icomply design, install and maintain industrial fire detection to BS 5839 for manufacturing and warehouse-linked production sites across the North West. From Stockport our engineers specify robust devices—heat, beam, aspirating or multi-sensor—interface to plant shutdowns and deliver commissioning that production managers can trust.
factory-fire-alarm-system.body: We survey mezzanines, racking and process lines for detector suitability, plan cable routes away from damage, and stage testing so production stoppages stay controlled. Cause-and-effect can include roller shutter release, extract isolation or process interlocks where the fire strategy requires it. Service contracts include industrial call-out options and spare device holding for critical lines.
factory-fire-alarm-system.meta_desc: Factory fire alarm system design & install for industrial sites. BS 5839, North West coverage — Icomply Stockport.
focus_points: Industrial detection selection for heat, dust and height
Plant interface and cause-and-effect programming
Phased install around production schedules
Robust servicing and reactive engineering support
faq: Which detectors suit factory floors?
It depends on the process. Heat, beam or aspirating systems often outperform standard optical smoke in dusty or high-ceiling spaces. We recommend after site survey.
Can the fire system shut down machinery?
Interfaces can signal plant or BMS for controlled shutdown where specified in the fire strategy. We coordinate with your electrical and process teams.
Do you cover multi-building industrial estates?

fire-alarm-battery-replacement.name: Fire Alarm Battery Replacement
fire-alarm-battery-replacement.intro: Fire alarm battery replacement restores standby power so panels and devices keep working during a mains failure. Icomply replaces sealed lead-acid and manufacturer-specified batteries on systems throughout the North West.
fire-alarm-battery-replacement.body: We size replacements to the original autonomy requirements, fit with correct polarity and fusing, then retest charge circuits. Ageing batteries are a leading cause of panel faults and failed inspections — proactive swaps during service visits prevent nuisance call-outs. Old batteries are disposed of responsibly.

Responsible disposal of spent batteries
faq: How often should fire alarm batteries be replaced?
Typically every three to five years depending on type, environment and manufacturer guidance — sooner if testing shows reduced capacity.
Why is my panel showing a battery fault?
Common causes include end-of-life cells, poor connections or charger faults. We diagnose before simply fitting new batteries.
Do you replace batteries for wireless field devices too?
Yes, where your system uses radio devices with local cells; these follow manufacturer intervals separate from panel standby batteries.

fire-alarm-certificate.name: Fire Alarm Certificate
fire-alarm-certificate.intro: A fire alarm certificate records that a system has been installed, commissioned or serviced to an agreed standard—typically with BS 5839 in mind for non-domestic and many multi-occupancy buildings. Icomply issues clear fire alarm certification after installation, modification and routine maintenance across Greater Manchester and the North West. From Stockport our engineers leave logbook entries, device counts and cause-and-effect notes that facilities managers and freeholders can show insurers and fire risk assessors.
fire-alarm-certificate.body: Certification without meaningful testing is worthless. How often should systems be certificated through service?
Many BS 5839 systems are serviced at least every six months by a competent person, with user tests more frequently—confirm against your FRA and insurer.
Can you certificate a system you did not install?
Yes. We service and certificate existing systems after inspection; major unknowns may need investigation before we sign a clean service record.

Which detector type suits a kitchen?

Can detection be added to an existing panel?

fire-door-inspection.name: Fire Door Inspection
fire-door-inspection.intro: Fire door inspection helps responsible persons prove that doors intended to hold back smoke and fire remain fit for purpose. Icomply Property Services support fire door inspection programmes across the North West with thorough condition surveys of door leaves, frames, seals, hinges, closers and ironmongery — reporting defects that undermine fire performance. From Stockport we work with landlords, agents and FM teams managing blocks and commercial premises, producing clear defect lists that feed FRA actions and remedial programmes.
fire-door-inspection.body: Inspections check gaps, seal condition, glazing units, signage, self-closing operation and unauthorised alterations such as added locks or damaged vision panels. Findings are prioritised so high-risk doors on escape routes are addressed first. We coordinate with fire alarm and emergency lighting teams where door issues sit within a wider life-safety package. Remedial works may involve specialists for certified door repairs or full leaf replacement depending on damage. Regular inspection cycles protect residents and support audit trails for enforcing authorities. Coverage includes Greater Manchester, Cheshire, Lancashire, Merseyside and Cumbria with multi-block packages for portfolio managers.
fire-door-inspection.meta_desc: Fire door inspection for blocks & commercial sites. Condition surveys, defect reports & FRA support. Icomply Stockport & North West.
focus_points: Systematic inspection of fire door leaves, frames, seals and closers
Prioritised defect reporting for escape routes and high-risk doors
Support for FRA actions and landlord compliance files
Coordination with wider fire safety remedial programmes
faq: How often should fire doors be inspected?
Frequency depends on building risk and guidance applicable to the premises. Many blocks adopt regular formal checks plus routine caretaker observations.
Can damaged fire doors be repaired?
Some defects are repairable with certified methods; others need full replacement. Our report explains practical options.
Do you certify fire doors as a manufacturer?
We inspect and report condition and can help arrange appropriate remediations. Certification of new door sets follows the product and installer route used for the works.

fire-rated-aov.name: Fire Rated AOV
fire-rated-aov.intro: Fire-rated AOV assemblies are specified where the vent or associated construction must maintain fire resistance as well as open for smoke control — product choice and installation detailing both matter. Icomply supply and install fire-rated AOV solutions where the design requires, and maintain them so seals, actuators and controls still perform, serving Greater Manchester and the North West from Stockport.
fire-rated-aov.body: We work to the fire strategy and manufacturer listings rather than substituting non-equivalent vents. Installation respects framing, fire-stopping and actuator fire performance requirements. After install, service visits check that fire-resisting features have not been compromised by weathering or ad-hoc repairs. If an existing ‘AOV’ lacks evidence of required fire performance, we flag the risk for your designer or fire engineer and quote compliant replacements where appropriate.
fire-rated-aov.meta_desc: Fire-rated AOV supply, install & maintain to design specification. Smoke control with fire performance. Manchester & North West.
focus_points: Product selection against fire strategy and listings
Careful install preserving fire-resisting details
Actuator and control integration without compromising rating
Inspection and maintenance that protects integrity
faq: Does every AOV need to be fire rated?
Not necessarily — requirements come from the building design and location of the vent. We install what the specification and strategy demand.
Can I upgrade a standard vent to fire rated?
Usually this means replacing with a listed assembly, not bolting on parts — we advise after inspection.
Do fire-rated AOVs need special maintenance?
They need the same operational tests plus attention to seals and any fire-resisting components that affect integrity.
fire-rated-aov.seo_keywords: fire rated AOV, fire-rated smoke vent, fire resisting AOV, fire rated automatic opening vent, fire rated AOV Manchester, fire rated AOV Stockport, EN 12101 fire rated vent, North West

fire-safety-order-compliance.name: Fire Safety Order Compliance
fire-safety-order-compliance.intro: Fire Safety Order compliance (Regulatory Reform (Fire Safety) Order 2005) requires a responsible person to assess risk and implement general fire precautions—including detection and warning where needed. Icomply help duty holders close fire alarm and emergency lighting gaps that undermine Order compliance across the North West. From Stockport we translate fire risk assessment actions into designed systems, maintenance regimes and records that stand up to audits and enforcement visits.
fire-safety-order-compliance.body: We do not replace your fire risk assessor; we implement and maintain the systems their actions require. faq: Does a fire alarm automatically make me compliant?
No. Compliance is wider than equipment. A suitable system, maintained and managed, supports your precautions but does not replace risk assessment or training.
Can you write my fire risk assessment?
We focus on systems installation and maintenance. We work from your FRA and can recommend independent assessors if you need one.
What records should I keep?
Risk assessment, system design/commissioning info, logbooks, service certificates and training records. We help keep the systems side complete.

fixed-wire-testing.name: Fixed Wire Testing
fixed-wire-testing.intro: Fixed wire testing — formal inspection and testing of electrical installations — underpins EICR compliance for landlords, businesses and facilities managers. Icomply Property Services carry out fixed wire testing to BS 7671 across Greater Manchester and the North West, producing clear condition reports, codes and remedial schedules. From Stockport our qualified electricians plan access, minimise downtime and explain findings in plain English so you can prioritise C1, C2 and C3 observations with confidence.
fixed-wire-testing.body: Fixed wire testing covers distribution boards, circuits, protective devices, earthing and bonding, and sample or full inspection of accessories depending on agreed scope and occupancy. We use calibrated instruments, isolate safely, and restore supplies methodically after tests. Commercial and multi-occupancy sites may need phased testing outside peak hours. After the visit you receive an Electrical Installation Condition Report with observations and a practical remedial quote where repairs are needed. Retests after remedials close the compliance loop. Related services include consumer unit upgrades, emergency lighting tests and landlord electrical packages. Coverage spans Manchester, Cheshire, Lancashire, Merseyside and Cumbria.
fixed-wire-testing.meta_desc: Fixed wire testing & EICR for landlords and commercial sites. BS 7671 reports. Icomply electricians — Stockport & North West.
focus_points: BS 7671 fixed wire testing and EICR production
Phased testing plans for occupied commercial and residential sites
Coded observations with practical remedial quotes
Board schedules, labelling improvements and compliance support
faq: Is fixed wire testing the same as an EICR?
The EICR is the report produced from inspection and testing of the fixed installation. People often use the terms together when booking compliance tests.
How often is fixed wire testing needed?
Intervals depend on premises type, use and previous report recommendations — often 5 years for many rentals and commercial sites, sometimes more frequent for higher-risk environments.
Will power be off during testing?
Circuits are isolated in sequence. We plan with you to reduce disruption and can work out of hours by agreement on commercial sites.

flue-installation.name: Flue Installation
flue-installation.intro: Correct flue installation is critical to boiler and water heater safety — poor routing, seals or terminals can cause carbon monoxide risk and failed inspections. Icomply design and install concentric, twin-pipe and conventional flue systems to manufacturer instructions and Gas Safe practice for homes and commercial plant rooms across the North West.
flue-installation.body: We survey roof, wall and riser routes for length limits, bends, plume nuisance, terminal positions and fire-stopping needs, then install with the correct brackets, seals and weatherproofing. Replacement flues on existing boilers are planned to minimise heat-off time, and new boiler packages include flue design as standard. After install we run combustion analysis, check draught and CO where required, and leave clear user guidance. Stockport-based engineers cover Greater Manchester and surrounding counties.
flue-installation.meta_desc: Flue installation & replacement for boilers and heaters. Gas Safe engineers across Manchester, Stockport & the North West.
focus_points: Manufacturer-compliant flue route design and terminal siting
Concentric, twin-pipe and conventional flue options
Weatherproofing, brackets and fire-stopping attention
Combustion checks and Gas Safe commissioning
faq: When does a flue need replacing?
Common reasons include corrosion, damaged seals, incorrect original installation, boiler upgrade to a different flue type, or failed safety inspection. We confirm after a site survey.
Can you install flues through shared roofs or multi-storey risers?
Yes where design and access allow. Shared or complex routes may need landlord approval, builders’ work openings and coordination with other trades — we flag this early.
Do you supply the flue materials?
Typically yes: flue kits, extensions, terminals and accessories are included in the fixed quote unless you specify supply-only or client-supplied parts.
flue-installation.seo_keywords: flue installation, boiler flue installation, flue replacement, concentric flue install, gas flue North West, flue installation Manchester, boiler flue Stockport

fob-access-control.name: Fob Access Control
fob-access-control.intro: Fob access control is a practical, familiar credential model for offices, apartments and managed blocks that want controlled entry without keys circulating between tenants. Icomply design, install, program and maintain fob access control systems across the North West, pairing readers, controllers and door hardware with fire-safe egress and clear admin processes for issuing and cancelling fobs.
fob-access-control.body: We help landlords and FM teams move from master-key chaos to auditable fob lists, time schedules and door groups. From our Stockport base we install new systems, expand existing ones and re-programme lost or stolen fobs quickly. Related searches often include proximity readers, multi-door control, visitor management and integrating fob entry with video door entry for residential blocks.
fob-access-control.meta_desc: Fob access control across Greater Manchester & the North West. Icomply install, program & maintain fob entry systems with fixed-price quotes.
focus_points: Fob-based access design for offices, blocks and estates
Reader and controller install with free-escape compliance
Fob programming, replacement and deactivation processes
Maintenance contracts and reactive access faults
faq: What happens if a tenant loses a fob?
We can re-program the system so the lost fob is deactivated and a replacement issued, subject to your admin process and system type.
Is fob access control suitable for apartment blocks?
Yes. Fobs are widely used on communal doors, bin stores and plant rooms when combined with correct fire egress and door entry design.
Can one fob work on multiple doors?
Usually yes — access levels can allow one credential on several doors or sites depending on the controller and software.

Typically a graded control panel, suitable detectors for doors, windows and interiors, power supply with battery backup, and user devices such as keypads or fobs. Exact devices follow the survey and any insurer requirements.
Do I need Grade 2 for a small shop or office?
Many commercial sites and some higher-risk homes are best served at Grade 2. We assess stock value, occupancy and access points, then recommend Grade 2 where it is proportionate and insurer-friendly.

faq: Is HD enough or do I need 4K CCTV?
For most doors, tills and yards, well-sited HD is sufficient and cheaper to store. We recommend higher resolution only where the risk assessment justifies the storage cost.
Can you replace old cameras with HD on existing cable?

heat-detector-installation.name: Heat Detector Installation
heat-detector-installation.intro: Heat detector installation is the sensible choice where smoke detectors would nuisance-alarm — kitchens, plant rooms and dusty spaces. Icomply installs rate-of-rise and fixed-temperature heat detection as part of BS 5839 systems across the North West.

faq: Can heat detectors replace all smoke detectors?
No. Heat detectors generally respond more slowly to smouldering fires and are used selectively where smoke detection is unsuitable.
Are heat detectors suitable for landlord kitchens?
Yes — heat detection is commonly used in kitchen areas within larger fire alarm or multi-sensor schemes.

We can add compatible devices if the panel and cabling support them, after checking loop loading and zoning.

high-risk-task-area-lighting.name: High Risk Task Area Lighting

Icomply design and install high-risk task area emergency lighting across Greater Manchester and the North West in line with BS 5266 principles. From Stockport we work with plant managers and duty holders to identify tasks that cannot simply stop in the dark—then engineer practical emergency cover.
high-risk-task-area-lighting.body: Examples include certain plant rooms, kitchens with hot processes, workshops and control points where residual hazards exist after mains loss. We assess tasks with the client, set appropriate emergency light levels and duration, and choose robust fittings that survive the environment. Integration with emergency stop strategies and fire procedures matters as much as lux figures. Testing regimes should prove that task lighting still hits the required level, not merely that an LED indicator is green. Documentation explains why higher levels were specified so future alterations do not quietly remove critical heads.
