faq: How do I know if I need high-risk task emergency lighting?
If a power cut leaves a hazardous task unfinished—hot oil, moving machinery, chemical processes—higher emergency lighting may be required. We assess with your team and FRA.
Is high-risk task lighting the same as normal escape lighting?
No. It typically demands higher illuminance focused on the task area, beyond basic escape route levels.
Can you add task emergency lighting to an existing system?
Yes. We can supplement existing escape lighting with additional heads or conversions targeted at the risk points.

intercom-battery-backup.name: Intercom Battery Backup
intercom-battery-backup.intro: Intercom battery backup keeps door entry and release working through short mains failures — critical for blocks, gates and commercial entrances that must not strand residents or staff. Does backup keep maglocks secure during a fire alarm?
Fire-release requirements override security locking. We design backup and interfaces so life safety behaviour remains correct.

Typically on a manufacturer-guided cycle or when capacity tests show decline — often every few years. Maintenance visits check health.

keypad-access-control.name: Keypad Access Control
keypad-access-control.intro: Keypad access control offers code-based entry for plant rooms, staff doors, plant compounds and smaller commercial premises that need better control than a mechanical key. Icomply install, program and maintain keypad access control systems across the North West, combining codes with electric locks or strikes and correct free-escape arrangements under BS EN 50133 and fire-release best practice.
keypad-access-control.body: Codes are simple to issue but need disciplined change processes when staff leave. We design keypads with weather rating where needed, anti-tamper considerations and optional dual-factor setups (code plus card). From Stockport we cover Greater Manchester, Lancashire, Cheshire, Merseyside and Cumbria for new installs, failed keypad replacements and upgrades to card or fob systems when code sharing becomes a risk.
keypad-access-control.meta_desc: Keypad access control across Greater Manchester & the North West. Icomply install & program code entry systems with fire-safe lock integration.

Electric lock or strike interface with free escape
Code programming, change procedures and user guidance
Upgrade paths from codes to card or multi-door control
faq: When is keypad access control the right choice?
Keypads suit lower-risk staff doors and plant rooms where codes can be managed tightly; higher-security sites often prefer cards or biometrics.
Can keypads work with maglocks?
Yes, when designed with correct power supplies, request-to-exit devices and fire-release so exit remains safe under alarm.
How often should door codes be changed?
We recommend changing codes when staff leave or periodically as part of your security policy; we can reprogram during service visits.

l1-fire-alarm-system.name: L1 Fire Alarm System
l1-fire-alarm-system.intro: An L1 fire alarm system provides the highest automatic detection coverage category under BS 5839 for life protection in non-domestic buildings—detection throughout escape routes and almost all rooms and areas. Icomply design, install and maintain L1 systems for high-risk or complex premises across Greater Manchester and the North West. From Stockport we deliver category justification, device schedules, commissioning and ongoing service so your fire strategy is evidence-based, not guesswork.
l1-fire-alarm-system.body: L1 is often specified for care, large public buildings or where the fire risk assessment demands maximum early warning. We balance coverage with false-alarm management using appropriate multi-sensor technology and zoning. Installation programmes, user training and six-monthly servicing keep L1 estates performing as designed.
l1-fire-alarm-system.meta_desc: L1 fire alarm system design, install & service to BS 5839. North West coverage from Stockport — Icomply.
focus_points: BS 5839 L1 category design and justification
Comprehensive detection and alarm coverage
Commissioning and cause-and-effect testing
Planned maintenance for high-integrity systems
faq: What does L1 mean?
L1 is a life-protection category with automatic detection in almost all areas plus escape routes, offering the most complete room coverage under BS 5839 Part 1.
Is L1 always required?
No. Category follows risk assessment and building use. We never oversell L1 where L2 or L3 meets the assessed need.
Can you upgrade from L3 to L1?
Yes, by extending detection into additional rooms and reviewing sounder coverage, power supplies and panel capacity as part of a staged upgrade.
l1-fire-alarm-system.seo_keywords: L1 fire alarm system, BS 5839 L1, L1 fire detection, category L1 install, L1 fire alarm Manchester, L1 fire alarm Stockport, North West, Icomply

l2-fire-alarm-system.name: L2 Fire Alarm System
l2-fire-alarm-system.intro: An L2 fire alarm system protects life with detection in escape routes plus defined high-risk rooms—such as plant rooms, kitchens or storage—identified by the fire risk assessment. Icomply specify and install L2 systems to BS 5839 for commercial and residential blocks across the North West. Our Stockport engineers survey risk rooms carefully so L2 delivers targeted coverage without unnecessary devices in low-risk spaces.
l2-fire-alarm-system.body: We document which rooms are included and why, programme the panel accordingly and leave charts that match the agreed category. L2 is a frequent choice where L1 would be excessive but L3 would leave critical rooms unprotected. Maintenance visits verify detectors in both escape routes and risk rooms remain clean and responsive.
l2-fire-alarm-system.meta_desc: L2 fire alarm system install & service to BS 5839. Escape routes plus risk rooms — Manchester & North West. Icomply.
focus_points: Risk-room identification with your FRA
Escape route and risk-area detection design
Install, commission and certificate
Category-aware planned maintenance
faq: How does L2 differ from L3?
L3 covers escape routes and rooms opening onto them; L2 adds selected high-risk rooms beyond that. Your fire risk assessment drives which rooms join the L2 set.
Who decides the risk rooms?
The responsible person and fire risk assessor lead; we implement detection that matches their documented strategy.
Can L2 be addressable or conventional?
Either technology can achieve L2 if coverage and performance meet BS 5839. We recommend based on size and future flexibility.
l2-fire-alarm-system.seo_keywords: L2 fire alarm system, BS 5839 L2, L2 fire detection, category L2 installation, L2 fire alarm Manchester, L2 fire alarm Stockport, North West, Icomply

l3-fire-alarm-system.name: L3 Fire Alarm System
l3-fire-alarm-system.intro: An L3 fire alarm system focuses life protection on escape routes and rooms that open onto those routes, giving early warning to support evacuation. Icomply design, install and service L3 systems to BS 5839 for offices, light commercial and many multi-occupancy buildings across Greater Manchester and the North West. From Stockport we provide honest category advice—L3 only where it fits the fire risk assessment—and fixed-price delivery with full documentation.
l3-fire-alarm-system.body: Typical L3 scopes include corridor and stair detection, rooms opening onto escapes, manual call points and adequate warning devices. We check door sets, voids and mezzanines that affect route protection, and integrate landlord systems where tenants share means of escape. Aftercare includes servicing and support for weekly tests by the duty holder.
l3-fire-alarm-system.meta_desc: L3 fire alarm system design & installation to BS 5839. North West offices & commercial sites — Icomply Stockport.
focus_points: Escape-route focused L3 design
Detector and MCP layout for corridors and adjoining rooms
Commissioning packs and zone charts
Service contracts and remedial support
faq: Is L3 enough for my building?
Many lower-risk commercial buildings use L3 successfully, but higher-risk uses may need L2 or L1. We align to your fire risk assessment rather than a one-size default.
Does L3 include the whole floor plate?
Not necessarily. Rooms that do not open onto escape routes may be omitted unless the FRA requires otherwise.
Can you certificate an existing L3 system?
We inspect and service existing systems, identify gaps against the claimed category, and quote upgrades if coverage falls short.
l3-fire-alarm-system.seo_keywords: L3 fire alarm system, BS 5839 L3, L3 fire detection, category L3 install, L3 fire alarm Manchester, L3 fire alarm Stockport, North West, Icomply

landlord-eicr.name: Landlord EICR
landlord-eicr.intro: Landlord EICR duties mean private rented properties in England generally need a valid Electrical Installation Condition Report, with copies available to tenants and local authorities on request. Icomply specialises in landlord EICR testing across Stockport, Greater Manchester and the wider North West—efficient access arrangements, clear reports, and remedial support so lettings are not left in limbo after an unsatisfactory result.
landlord-eicr.body: We work daily with landlords and letting agents: void-period testing, mid-tenancy access where agreed, and portfolio schedules that keep multiple addresses on a rolling plan. Reports highlight what must be fixed before you can confidently re-let or demonstrate compliance. When C1 or C2 codes appear, our electricians can quote and complete remedials, then provide the paperwork trail agents expect. Combine EICR visits with smoke alarm checks or consumer unit upgrades when you want one coordinated compliance package.
landlord-eicr.meta_desc: Landlord EICR testing & reports across Greater Manchester & the North West. Agent-friendly BS 7671 inspections from Icomply Stockport.
focus_points: Private rental EICRs aligned to landlord electrical safety rules
Agent and portfolio booking with flexible access
Plain-English codes and prioritised remedials
Supporting certificates after fixed wiring repairs
faq: How often do landlords need an EICR?
For most private rentals in England, at least every five years or at a change of tenancy if the existing report has expired—unless the previous report recommends a shorter interval.
What if my landlord EICR is unsatisfactory?
You should arrange remedial works for dangerous and potentially dangerous faults within the expected timescales. We can complete those works and document completion.
Can you email the report to my agent?
Yes. We supply digital copies to landlords and nominated agents so tenancy files stay complete.
landlord-eicr.seo_keywords: landlord EICR, rental property EICR, landlord electrical certificate, private rented EICR Manchester, letting agent EICR Stockport, North West landlord testing

landlord-electrical-certificate.name: Landlord Electrical Certificate
landlord-electrical-certificate.intro: A landlord electrical certificate usually means a current EICR plus any works certificates needed to prove rental properties meet electrical safety duties. iComply helps landlords obtain landlord electrical certificates with coded reports and remedial completion where required.
landlord-electrical-certificate.body: We understand tenancy turnaround pressure and can target voids or coordinate tenanted access. Reports are written so agents can file them without chasing missing schedules. Register your portfolio for recurring five-year cycles and change-of-tenancy checks.

focus_points: EICRs aligned to private rental duties

Portfolio scheduling for multiple units
faq: How quickly must remedials be done after an unsatisfactory report?
Dangerous conditions need immediate action. Other codes have recommended timescales; we help you plan against risk and legal expectations.
Do furnished rentals also need PAT?
Portable appliances supplied with the let should be maintained safely. PAT is a practical way to evidence that duty alongside the fixed-wire EICR.
Can one certificate cover a whole HMO?
An EICR covers the installation inspected. HMOs may need careful scope definition for landlord and tenant supplies—we clarify boundaries on site.

landlord-electrical-safety-check.name: Landlord Electrical Safety Check
landlord-electrical-safety-check.intro: A landlord electrical safety check is the practical visit that underpins your legal duty to keep rental electrics safe—usually delivered as a full EICR with follow-on repairs. Icomply helps landlords and letting agents across Greater Manchester and the North West complete electrical safety checks on time, with tenant-aware access and straightforward reporting. From Stockport we keep the process efficient so voids are not stretched and occupied tenancies are disrupted only as long as testing requires.
landlord-electrical-safety-check.body: Checks include the consumer unit, visible wiring, sockets, switches, fixed equipment connections and earthing arrangements within scope. We can also verify smoke and CO alarm presence if requested as part of a wider safety visit, though fire detection is separate from the EICR itself. Remedial works are quoted clearly against codes. faq: Is a landlord electrical safety check legally required?
In England, private landlords must ensure installations are inspected and tested at required intervals and meet standards—commonly evidenced by an EICR.
Do I need a check between tenancies?
If your existing report is still valid and covers the installation, you may not need a new one at every change—but many agents prefer a fresh check on older properties.
Can you check multiple rentals in one day?
Yes when properties are nearby and access is arranged—ideal for local portfolios around Greater Manchester.

Icomply deliver landlord emergency lighting installation, monthly and annual testing and remedial upgrades to BS 5266 across Stockport, Greater Manchester and the North West, with certificates and logbooks suitable for agents, freeholders and insurance reviews.
landlord-emergency-lighting.body: We work on HMOs, purpose-built blocks, mixed-use retail/residential and small commercial lets where the responsible person must show escape lighting is maintained. Services include bulkhead and exit-sign replacement, LED conversions, duration testing, photo evidence of failed fittings and prioritised remedials so voids and tenancy turnovers are not delayed. Multi-property packages keep several addresses on one schedule. Tell us the postcode, number of floors and any existing test regime for a clear fixed-price proposal.

LED upgrades and exit-route remedials
Agent-friendly certificates and multi-site contracts
faq: Is emergency lighting a landlord responsibility?

Where the landlord or freeholder is the responsible person for common parts, they must ensure emergency lighting is suitable, maintained and tested — usually under fire safety duties and BS 5266 good practice.
How often should landlord emergency lighting be tested?
Typically a short monthly function test and a full-duration annual test, with records retained for audit.
Do you cover multiple rental properties?
Yes. We offer multi-property packages across the North West with shared scheduling and consistent paperwork for agents.

landlord-safety-certificate.name: Landlord Safety Certificate
landlord-safety-certificate.intro: A landlord safety certificate for electrical installations gives tenants, agents and insurers confidence that rented property meets current expectations. Icomply carries out EICR-based assessments and related remedial works for private landlords and letting agents across the North West.
landlord-safety-certificate.body: Our engineers inspect fixed wiring, consumer units, earthing and bonding, then grade observations so you know what is urgently required. Clear certificates and schedules make portfolio management simpler, and we can quote remediations as a fixed package after the inspection. Plain-English defect codes and priority guidance
Remedial electrical works quoted after inspection
Certificate packs suitable for tenancy files and insurers
faq: How often do landlords need an electrical safety certificate?

In England, private landlords must ensure electrical installations are inspected and tested at least every five years, or at each new tenancy if the existing report has expired, unless a shorter interval is stated on the report.
Is a landlord safety certificate the same as an EICR?
Yes in practice — the Electrical Installation Condition Report is the standard document used to evidence electrical safety for rented homes.
Can you certificate multiple rental properties?
We regularly schedule portfolio days for agents and landlords with several addresses across Greater Manchester and nearby counties.

life-safety-ventilation.name: Life Safety Ventilation
life-safety-ventilation.intro: Looking for life-safety ventilation that protects escape routes and supports fire-fighter access? Icomply install, commission and maintain life-safety smoke ventilation—natural AOVs, mechanical extract and control systems—for buildings across Greater Manchester and the North West. These systems are not comfort HVAC; they are critical safety assets that must be tested, powered and documented like other fire provisions.
life-safety-ventilation.body: Life-safety ventilation keeps stairs, corridors, lobbies and sometimes car parks tenable under design fire conditions. We deliver the hardware and controls that implement your fire strategy: vents, actuators, fans, dampers, panels, manual overrides and standby power. Commissioning proves modes and interfaces; maintenance keeps performance from drifting between audits. Defects are reported with clear priorities for responsible persons under building safety duties. Whether you manage a single block or a regional portfolio, our Stockport team provides fixed-price surveys, installation programmes and multi-site service contracts covering Greater Manchester, Cheshire, Lancashire, Merseyside and beyond.
life-safety-ventilation.meta_desc: Life-safety ventilation install, test & maintain. AOV and mechanical smoke control across Greater Manchester & the North West.
focus_points: Natural and mechanical life-safety ventilation installation
Cause-and-effect commissioning with standby power verification
Planned maintenance and certification for responsible persons
Portfolio programmes for multi-building landlords and FM teams
faq: Is life-safety ventilation the same as air conditioning?
No. Life-safety systems are designed for smoke control and escape route protection, with supervised controls and standby power, not everyday comfort cooling.
What standards apply?
Design is led by the fire strategy; equipment and practice commonly reference BS 9991 for many residential cases and EN 12101 for smoke and heat control products/systems.
How do I know if my building’s system is adequate?
Start with the fire strategy and a functional test. We report whether installed equipment operates as intended and flag gaps for your fire engineer or responsible person.
life-safety-ventilation.seo_keywords: life safety ventilation, life-safety smoke ventilation, smoke control ventilation, AOV life safety, mechanical smoke ventilation North West, life safety ventilation Manchester

louvre-aov.name: Louvre AOV
louvre-aov.intro: Louvre AOVs provide free area for smoke control through multi-blade louvre banks that open automatically on fire signal. Icomply install and service louvre AOV systems for plant rooms, shafts, façades and stair ventilation across Stockport, Greater Manchester and the wider North West, including actuators, controls and weather performance checks.
louvre-aov.body: Louvre systems need blades that move freely, linkages that stay aligned and motors with enough torque for the full bank. We install new louvre AOVs, replace failed drives, clear debris that blocks blades and commission open percentage against design. Maintenance contracts reduce the risk of seized blades after winter weather. Fixed-price scopes follow measurement of opening size and review of the existing control arrangement.
louvre-aov.meta_desc: Louvre AOV install & service — multi-blade smoke vents, actuators & controls. Stockport, Manchester & North West — Icomply.
focus_points: Louvre bank AOV specification and installation
Linkage and actuator torque checks
Debris, weather and free-movement maintenance
Control-panel proving for full open position
faq: Why choose louvre AOVs?
They can deliver large free areas on façades or plant enclosures while controlling weather entry when closed.
What usually fails on louvre AOVs?
Seized blades, corroded linkages, underpowered actuators and battery/panel faults are common.
Can you motorise existing manual louvres?
Sometimes, if structure and free area suit — we assess and quote rather than assuming every louvre can convert.
louvre-aov.seo_keywords: louvre AOV, AOV louvre, smoke louvre, automatic louvre vent, louvre AOV Manchester, louvre AOV Stockport, façade smoke louvre, North West louvre AOV

magnetic-lock-installation.name: Magnetic Lock Installation
magnetic-lock-installation.intro: Magnetic lock installation secures doors with fail-safe holding force when paired correctly with access control, power supplies and fire-release. Icomply design and install maglocks for commercial and multi-tenant doors across Greater Manchester and the North West, ensuring free escape, monitoring contacts and commissioning documentation that facilities teams can rely on.
magnetic-lock-installation.body: A maglock is only as safe as its egress design: break-glass or fire-panel release, request-to-exit, battery backup and door geometry all matter. Our engineers survey the door leaf, frame and traffic pattern before fitting brackets, armature plates and cable routes that last. Based in Stockport, we also replace failing magnets, upgrade ageing PSUs and integrate maglocks with Paxton, HID-compatible and other access platforms.
magnetic-lock-installation.meta_desc: Magnetic lock installation across Greater Manchester & the North West. Icomply fit maglocks with fire-release, egress devices and fixed-price quotes.
focus_points: Survey of door type, holding force and mounting method
Maglock, PSU, RTE and fire-release integration
Access control interface and door monitoring where required
Replacement magnets and remedial works on existing doors
faq: Are magnetic locks allowed on fire exit doors?
They can be used when designed fail-safe with correct fire-panel or emergency release so occupants can escape — we design to fire-release best practice.
Do maglocks need battery backup?
A suitable power supply with battery standby is best practice so doors remain controlled during short mains failures while still releasing on fire signal where required.
Can you install maglocks on double doors?
Yes. We specify brackets and holding arrangements suitable for double-leaf and glass doors after inspection.
magnetic-lock-installation.seo_keywords: magnetic lock installation, maglock install, electromagnetic lock fitting, maglock access control, magnetic door lock Manchester, maglock fire release Stockport, commercial maglock North West, access control locks

maintained-emergency-lighting.name: Maintained Emergency Lighting
maintained-emergency-lighting.intro: Maintained emergency lighting operates as normal lighting or continuously lit signage and still provides emergency output on mains failure—essential for many exit signs and areas needing constant visual guidance. Icomply install and service maintained emergency lighting across Greater Manchester and the North West to BS 5266. From Stockport we configure LED maintained fittings that stay clear, efficient and testable for commercial and multi-residential buildings.
maintained-emergency-lighting.body: Maintained exit signs must remain legible, correctly oriented and unobscured by décor or stock. We position legends for viewing distances, select appropriate formats and ensure emergency drivers and batteries are healthy. Maintained route lighting can also serve dual-purpose illumination in some designs. Service visits check both everyday operation and emergency transfer, because a sign can look fine on mains yet fail when the supply drops. Upgrading old maintained fluorescent boxes to LED often improves clarity and cuts energy while simplifying spares across an estate.
maintained-emergency-lighting.meta_desc: Maintained emergency lighting & exit signs across GM & the North West. BS 5266 install, service & testing from Icomply Stockport. Fixed-price quotes.
focus_points: Maintained exit signs and dual-purpose emergency luminaires
Correct legends, mounting and viewing performance
LED upgrades for clarity and lower energy use
Transfer and duration testing on service programmes
faq: Do all emergency lights need to be maintained?
No. Many route lights are non-maintained; exit signs are commonly maintained. Design follows BS 5266 and site needs.
Why is my maintained exit sign flickering?
Driver, LED board or supply issues may be to blame. We diagnose and replace modules rather than ignoring early warnings.
Can maintained signs run from a central battery?
Yes on central systems; self-contained maintained signs are also widely used. We specify the architecture that suits the building.
maintained-emergency-lighting.seo_keywords: maintained emergency lighting, maintained emergency lights, maintained exit sign, maintained emergency lighting North West, maintained emergency lighting Manchester, BS 5266 maintained, emergency lighting Stockport

manual-call-point.name: Manual Call Point
manual-call-point.intro: A manual call point lets occupants raise a fire alarm instantly on escape routes and exits. Icomply supplies, sites and replaces call points on conventional and addressable systems across the North West.
manual-call-point.body: We position MCP units at the correct heights and locations under BS 5839 guidance, with clear visibility and resettable elements where preferred. Weatherproof versions protect external exits, while transparent covers can reduce malicious activations in public areas. Each device is tested into the panel and logged.
manual-call-point.meta_desc: Manual call point supply, siting & replacement across Stockport, Greater Manchester & the North West. BS 5839 MCP work by Icomply.
focus_points: Correct MCP siting on escape routes and storey exits
Resettable and break-glass options, including covers
Weatherproof call points for external doors
Addressable and conventional wiring or loop connection
faq: How high should a manual call point be mounted?
BS 5839 typically recommends a mounting height around 1.2 m to the operating element, subject to accessibility needs.
Can call point covers stop false alarms?
Hinged covers reduce accidental or malicious activations in many public buildings and are commonly specified.
Do you replace damaged call points quickly?
Yes — we stock common MCP types for popular systems and can often fit replacements on a short-notice visit.

mechanical-smoke-ventilation.name: Mechanical Smoke Ventilation
mechanical-smoke-ventilation.intro: Need mechanical smoke ventilation design support, installation or maintenance? Icomply deliver powered smoke control solutions—fans, dampers, controls and interfaces—for corridors, stairs, car parks and complex residential cores across the North West. When natural AOVs cannot achieve the required performance, mechanical systems extract or pressurise to protect escape routes under the approved strategy.
mechanical-smoke-ventilation.body: Mechanical smoke ventilation combines heavy electrical plant with life-safety controls. We install and commission extract fans, smoke dampers, speed controls, panels and fire alarm interfaces, then verify cause-and-effect with measured operation as scoped. Service includes fan run tests, damper travel, panel batteries, clean filter checks where applicable and documented certificates. natural-smoke-ventilation.name: Natural Smoke Ventilation
natural-smoke-ventilation.intro: Natural smoke ventilation uses wind and buoyancy through AOVs, roof vents and façade openings rather than large mechanical fans — a common approach for residential stair cores. Icomply design-support, install and maintain natural smoke ventilation systems across Stockport and the North West, ensuring vents open reliably to the free areas your design requires.
natural-smoke-ventilation.body: Success depends on correctly sized openings, reliable actuators, clear airflow paths and disciplined maintenance. We fit roof and window vents, louvre banks, controls and battery systems, then commission open/close performance under real conditions. Existing natural systems that stick shut or leak weather are surveyed for repair or upgrade. Landlords appreciate plain-English reports that explain why a seized vent is a life-safety issue, not a cosmetic defect.
natural-smoke-ventilation.meta_desc: Natural smoke ventilation via AOVs & roof vents. Install, test & maintain. BS 9991 practice. Manchester, Stockport & North West.
focus_points: Natural smoke ventilation for stairs and lobbies
Roof, window and louvre vent installation
Free-area equipment matched to design intent
Maintenance that keeps vents free to open fully
faq: Is natural smoke ventilation enough for my building?
That is a design decision based on height, layout and fire strategy — we implement and maintain natural systems where they are specified.
Do natural vents need power?
Actuated vents need power and usually battery backup even though the smoke movement itself is natural.
What maintenance do natural smoke vents need?
Regular open/close tests, seal and linkage checks, battery service and confirmation of fire-alarm interfaces.
natural-smoke-ventilation.seo_keywords: natural smoke ventilation, natural smoke vent system, natural AOV, residential natural smoke ventilation, smoke ventilation Manchester, natural smoke vents Stockport, BS 9991 natural ventilation, North West AOV

new-boiler-quote.name: New Boiler Quote
new-boiler-quote.intro: A clear new boiler quote should cover the appliance, flue, controls, system clean, labour, disposal and certification — not a vague headline price. Icomply survey properties across Greater Manchester and the North West to specify the right output and flue type, then provide fixed-price new boiler quotes with Gas Safe installation and handover paperwork.
new-boiler-quote.body: We assess heat loss indicators, hot water demand, cylinder or combi suitability, gas supply and flue routes, then recommend reliable brands suited to your budget and property. Quotes can include magnetic filtration, power flush, smart controls and radiator upgrades so you compare like with like. Finance options may be discussed where available; all installs include commissioning, Benchmark-style records where applicable and user demonstration. Start with photos and your postcode for a desktop estimate, confirmed on survey from Stockport.

Fixed-price packages including labour and commissioning
Optional power flush, filters and smart controls
Gas Safe install with full user handover
faq: How much does a new boiler cost?
Prices depend on boiler type, flue complexity, system condition and extras such as filters or power flush. We provide a fixed quote after survey rather than a one-size online figure.
Combi or system boiler — which do I need?
Combis suit many homes without a hot water cylinder; system or regular boilers suit higher simultaneous hot water demand or existing cylinder setups. We recommend based on your usage and pipework.
How long does installation take?
A straightforward like-for-like combi swap is often completed in a day; complex flue changes, rewires of controls or system redesigns can take longer. Timescales are stated on your quote.

Mixed systems are common: maintained exit signs for visibility with non-maintained route lighting alongside. During service we verify that non-maintained fittings still transfer cleanly on supply failure and achieve duration. If your building has confusing mixed circuits or unlabelled key switches, we rationalise identification as part of compliance works.

Typically along many internal escape routes and areas where lights are not required to show an exit legend continuously—final design follows BS 5266 and the fire strategy.

Failed batteries, open lamps/LEDs, wiring faults or incorrect maintained/non-maintained configuration are common causes; we diagnose and repair.

open-area-emergency-lighting.name: Open Area Emergency Lighting
open-area-emergency-lighting.intro: Open area emergency lighting—sometimes called anti-panic lighting—helps people move safely across larger spaces to reach defined escape routes when normal power fails. Icomply specify and install open area emergency lighting across the North West for halls, open-plan offices, retail floors and similar spaces under BS 5266. From Stockport we ensure coverage is even enough to prevent panic without over-lighting or glare.
open-area-emergency-lighting.body: Open areas fail compliance when only perimeter exit signs are fitted and the centre of the room drops into darkness. We map furniture layouts, mezzanines and obstructions, then place luminaires to achieve suitable emergency illuminance. LED solutions reduce load and simplify battery maintenance compared with older gear trays. Systems are commissioned with records that distinguish open area fittings from pure escape route heads, helping future engineers maintain the design intent. For multi-use halls we also consider high-risk tasks that may need higher emergency levels when activities warrant it.
open-area-emergency-lighting.meta_desc: Open area emergency lighting (anti-panic) across GM & the North West. BS 5266 design & install from Icomply Stockport. Surveys, LED kits & certificates.
focus_points: Open area / anti-panic coverage design for large spaces
Even emergency illuminance to reach escape routes
LED fittings suited to halls, offices and retail floors
Commissioning records that preserve design intent
faq: Do small rooms need open area emergency lighting?
Small rooms may be covered by escape route principles or local design judgement; larger open spaces more often need dedicated open area lighting under BS 5266 guidance.
Can existing downlights be converted for open area emergency use?
Some luminaires accept emergency conversion modules if optical performance remains adequate; otherwise dedicated emergency heads are specified.
Will open area lights stay on all the time?
They may be maintained or non-maintained depending on design. We configure operation to match use of the space.

panel-upgrade.name: Panel Upgrade
panel-upgrade.intro: A panel upgrade—replacing or expanding a consumer unit or distribution board—improves protection, capacity and clarity of circuit labelling. iComply carries out panel upgrades when breakers are obsolete, RCDs are missing or additional ways are needed for modern loads.
panel-upgrade.body: We photograph and map existing circuits, plan changeover downtime and test every outgoing circuit before sign-off. Metal boards, dual RCD or all-RCBO layouts are chosen against risk and budget. Send a clear photo of your current board interior to begin scoping.
panel-upgrade.meta_desc: Consumer unit and distribution panel upgrade in the North West. Safer protection, more ways, full testing.
focus_points: Consumer unit and three-phase board upgrades
RCBO and RCD protection strategies
Circuit identification and new labelling
Minimised downtime changeover plans
faq: How long will power be off during a panel upgrade?
Domestic upgrades often need several hours of isolation. Commercial boards may be phased by section if the installation allows.
Must all circuits be rewired for a new board?
No. A panel upgrade re-terminates existing circuits if they are sound. Defective circuits found during testing are quoted separately.
Are old rewirable fuse boards still acceptable?
They rarely meet modern expectations for additional protection. Upgrading is strongly recommended for safety and insurance comfort.
panel-upgrade.seo_keywords: panel upgrade, consumer unit upgrade, fuse board replacement, distribution board upgrade, panel upgrade North West

part-p-certified.name: Part P Certified
part-p-certified.intro: Part P certified electricians understand the Building Regulations requirements that apply to electrical work in dwellings in England. iComply undertakes Part P relevant works with self-certification routes where scheme membership allows, reducing hassle for homeowners and landlords.
part-p-certified.body: Not all electrical tasks are notifiable, but new circuits, consumer unit changes and work in bathrooms often are. We advise which jobs need notification and complete the process as part of the package. Describe your planned kitchen or bathroom project and we will map Part P duties before work starts.
part-p-certified.meta_desc: Part P certified electrical works for dwellings in the North West. Notifiable jobs handled with correct certs.
focus_points: Domestic notifiable work guidance
Consumer unit and new circuit compliance
Bathroom and kitchen special locations
Self-certification where scheme rules allow
faq: What is Part P in simple terms?
Part P is the Building Regulations part covering electrical safety in dwellings. It aims to ensure work is designed and installed to avoid danger.
Do like-for-like socket swaps need Part P notification?
Many minor like-for-like replacements are non-notifiable, but condition and earthing still matter. We confirm classification per job.
What if a DIY relative already started the wiring?
We can inspect and test, but we only certify work that meets standards. Rework may be required before certification is possible.
part-p-certified.seo_keywords: Part P certified, Part P electrician, Building Regulations electrical, notifiable electrical work, Part P certified North West

partial-rewire.name: Partial Rewire
partial-rewire.intro: A partial rewire renews selected circuits or areas—kitchens, bathrooms, extensions or deteriorated loft runs—without stripping every wall in the building. Icomply plans partial rewires where testing shows localised risk or a refurbishment creates new demand, working to BS 7671 across Greater Manchester and the North West. From Stockport we survey first, map what stays and what goes, and quote a defined scope so you are not sold a full rewire you do not need.
partial-rewire.body: Partial rewires suit properties with mixed-age wiring: sound lighting circuits upstairs, for example, but unsafe socket circuits in a wet room. We isolate works carefully, update the consumer unit when protection needs changing, and certificate the altered circuits properly. faq: When is a partial rewire better than a full rewire?

Yes. Room-by-room programmes are common on occupied homes; we keep temporary safe supplies where practical between stages.

Do doors unlock when the fire alarm activates?
Where required by the fire strategy, we wire fail-safe release or interface modules so escape routes remain available during an alarm.

pd-6662-alarm-system.name: PD 6662 Alarm System
pd-6662-alarm-system.intro: PD 6662 is the UK application of European intruder and hold-up alarm standards — the framework insurers and professional installers expect. Maintenance contracts keep batteries, sensors and signalling healthy between visits.

faq: What does PD 6662 mean for my site?
It sets how European alarm standards are applied in the UK, including grading and installation practice. Following it helps with insurer confidence and professional monitoring options.
Is every alarm sold as PD 6662 compliant?
No. DIY and ungraded kits may not meet the standard. We specify and install equipment and methods intended to satisfy PD 6662 requirements for your agreed grade.

pd6662-alarm-system.name: PD6662 Alarm System
pd6662-alarm-system.intro: PD 6662 is the UK scheme that aligns intruder and hold-up alarm systems with European grading under BS EN 50131. When you ask for a PD6662 alarm system, you want design, install and documentation that stand up to insurers, police response criteria and professional maintenance. Icomply delivers PD 6662-focused installs and upgrades from our Stockport base, covering Greater Manchester and the wider North West with practical, site-specific engineering rather than catalogue kits alone.

faq: What is a PD6662 alarm system?

It is an intruder (and where required hold-up) alarm installed and maintained in line with the UK PD 6662 scheme and the relevant European standards, including graded equipment and proper documentation.
Does PD 6662 guarantee police response?

Yes. Commissioning records, grade information and handover notes are provided as part of a professional PD 6662-aligned installation.

periodic-electrical-inspection.name: Periodic Electrical Inspection
periodic-electrical-inspection.intro: A periodic electrical inspection is the scheduled health check of an existing installation—delivered today as an EICR under BS 7671. Icomply provides periodic electrical inspection for landlords, facilities managers and homeowners who need formal proof that fixed wiring remains safe for continued use. Covering Stockport, Greater Manchester and the wider North West, we combine thorough testing with practical advice on when to repair, upgrade or simply re-inspect at the next interval.
periodic-electrical-inspection.body: Periodic inspection is not the same as PAT testing of appliances or a quick visual look around a board. We examine distribution equipment, wiring systems, accessories and earthing arrangements, then test circuits systematically. Findings are coded so you can prioritise safety-critical items. Many clients align periodic electrical inspection with insurance renewals, lease events or five-year landlord cycles. We diary next due dates and can coordinate related works such as emergency lighting tests or consumer unit upgrades during the same visit window where useful.
periodic-electrical-inspection.meta_desc: Periodic electrical inspection (EICR) across Greater Manchester & the North West. BS 7671 testing & reports from Icomply Stockport.
focus_points: Scheduled periodic inspection to BS 7671 (EICR)
Safety coding that supports maintenance budgets
Suitable for domestic, rental and commercial estates
Next-inspection recommendations recorded on every report
faq: Is periodic inspection the same as an EICR?
Yes in modern practice—the Electrical Installation Condition Report is the document produced after periodic inspection and testing of an existing installation.
Can you inspect only the landlord’s supply in a block?
Scope can be limited to landlord areas if defined clearly. Tenant demise circuits may need separate access and agreements.
Do you provide a schedule of test results?
Reports include the formal condition assessment and supporting test data as required for a complete EICR package.
periodic-electrical-inspection.seo_keywords: periodic electrical inspection, periodic inspection testing, fixed wire inspection, EICR periodic, electrical inspection Manchester, BS 7671 Stockport

periodic-emergency-lighting-test.name: Periodic Emergency Lighting Test
periodic-emergency-lighting-test.intro: A periodic emergency lighting test is the scheduled check—function or full duration—that proves escape lighting still works between major upgrades. Icomply deliver periodic emergency lighting testing across the North West for landlords, care providers and commercial FM teams. Based in Stockport and guided by BS 5266, we run tests, record results and fix failures so compliance does not rely on hope.
periodic-emergency-lighting-test.body: Periodic testing usually combines client monthly function checks with competent annual duration tests, though higher-risk sites may need more frequent professional input. We diary visits, minimise disruption, restore charging and issue clear pass/fail data. Failures feed straight into repair or battery programmes with fixed prices. Multi-site portfolios can share a rolling test calendar so not every building discharges on the same week. Where self-testing fittings are installed, periodic professional review still validates that automation and physical condition remain trustworthy.
periodic-emergency-lighting-test.meta_desc: Periodic emergency lighting tests to BS 5266 across GM & the North West. Function & duration programmes from Icomply Stockport. Records & remedials.
focus_points: Scheduled function and annual duration test programmes
Building-by-building results with failure prioritisation
Remedial repairs quoted from test data
Rolling multi-site calendars for estates teams
faq: What is included in a periodic emergency lighting test visit?
Agreed test type (function or duration), inspection of obvious defects, recording of results and recommendations for failed fittings.
Can tenants stay in the building during duration tests?
Usually yes with planning. We manage discharge windows and advise the responsible person on any temporary residual risk.
Do you remind us when the next periodic test is due?
Service clients receive scheduling support so annual tests do not slip unnoticed.

periodic-fire-alarm-service.name: Periodic Fire Alarm Service
periodic-fire-alarm-service.intro: Periodic fire alarm service keeps detection, sounders, power supplies and user controls reliable between major life-cycle upgrades. Icomply schedule and deliver BS 5839-aligned periodic servicing for landlords, care homes and commercial premises across the North West. From Stockport our engineers plan visits around occupancy, complete structured checks, and leave certificates and logbook entries that stand up to fire risk assessments and insurer reviews.
periodic-fire-alarm-service.body: A proper periodic service is more than a quick panel reset. We inspect control equipment, batteries, chargers, detectors and manual call points on a risk-based sampling plan, test cause-and-effect where required, and flag devices that need cleaning, replacement or re-siting. Findings are recorded clearly so responsible persons know what is advisory versus urgent. Contracts can align with quarterly, six-monthly or manufacturer-recommended intervals and can sit alongside emergency lighting and other life-safety PPMs. Multi-site clients get consistent reporting formats so estates teams can compare condition across buildings without digging through paper folders.
periodic-fire-alarm-service.meta_desc: Periodic fire alarm service to BS 5839 across Greater Manchester & the North West. Planned visits, certificates & logbooks from Icomply Stockport. Free quotes.
focus_points: Scheduled periodic visits aligned to BS 5839 and site risk
Battery, charger, device and panel functional checks with written findings
Service certificates and fire logbook updates after each visit
Optional multi-site PPM packages with consistent reporting
faq: How often should a fire alarm have a periodic service?

Most commercial systems need competent person inspection at intervals set out in BS 5839 and your fire risk assessment—commonly every six months as a minimum, with more frequent attention for higher-risk or complex premises.
Is periodic service the same as a weekly user test?

No. Weekly tests are typically a responsible-person check of a call point and indicators. Periodic service is a competent engineer’s deeper inspection, testing and documentation of the installed system.
Can you take over periodic servicing from another contractor?
Yes. We review existing logbooks, zone charts and panel history, then set a realistic service plan and catch up on overdue devices or missing documentation.

periodic-inspection-report.intro: A periodic inspection report—commonly delivered today as an EICR—records the condition of an electrical installation at set intervals. iComply produces periodic inspection reports that facilities managers can action, not just file.
periodic-inspection-report.body: Sampling strategies, limitation statements and observation codes are explained so you know what was and was not covered. Trends across multi-year reports help justify capital upgrades to boards and submains. Align periodic dates with other compliance visits to cut access costs.

focus_points: BS 7671 periodic inspection methodology
Clear limitation and extent statements
Coded observations with practical priorities
Scheduling tied to portfolio compliance calendars
faq: Why do reports list limitations?
Some areas are unsafe or impossible to access fully. Honest limitations protect you from assuming untested circuits were proven sound.
What interval should commercial sites use?
Risk, environment and previous report advice drive intervals—often three to five years, sometimes less for harsh conditions.
Can you compare this report with our last one?
Yes. Bring prior paperwork and we will highlight recurring issues and completed remedials.

pir-security-lighting.name: PIR Security Lighting
pir-security-lighting.intro: Searching for PIR security lighting that only illuminates when movement is detected? Icomply install PIR-controlled LED security lighting for pathways, side elevations, bin stores, plant enclosures and residential blocks across the North West. We position sensors to avoid nuisance triggers from roads or wildlife, install robust luminaires, and commission detection ranges so landlords and commercial clients get reliable night-time coverage without constant energy waste.
pir-security-lighting.body: Based in Stockport (SK2), our engineers cover Greater Manchester, Lancashire, Cheshire, Merseyside and Cumbria. PIR security lighting suits areas that are quiet most of the night but need instant light for staff, residents or cameras when someone approaches. We select sensors with adjustable time and lux thresholds, combine them with photocells where required, and use IP-rated LED bulkheads or floods matched to the mounting surface. Fixed-price quotes follow a short survey; remedials include repositioning badly sited PIRs that false-trigger or leave dark pockets. Related services include full external security schemes and emergency lighting at final exits.
pir-security-lighting.meta_desc: PIR security lighting install across Greater Manchester & the North West. Motion-sensor LED lights, fixed quotes from Icomply Stockport.
focus_points: Survey and fixed-price quote for PIR security lighting
Correct sensor siting to cut nuisance trips and dark spots
LED bulkheads and floods with adjustable time and lux settings
Install, commission and multi-unit programmes for landlords and FM
faq: Why does my PIR light keep coming on?
Common causes are sensors aimed at roads, trees or heat sources; we realign or upgrade detection so triggers match genuine movement on site.
Can PIR lights work with CCTV?
Yes. Well-aimed PIR lighting improves night images; we avoid direct glare into camera lenses during design.
Are PIR fittings suitable outdoors?
We specify outdoor-rated luminaires and sensors with appropriate IP ratings for façades, yards and pathways.
pir-security-lighting.seo_keywords: PIR security lighting, motion sensor security lights, PIR LED bulkhead, outdoor PIR lighting install, PIR lights Manchester, Stockport PIR lighting, commercial PIR security lights North West

pir-sensor-installation.name: PIR Sensor Installation
pir-sensor-installation.intro: Passive infrared (PIR) sensors are the workhorses of room protection — detecting body heat movement while you are away. Icomply install, replace and re-site PIR sensors on domestic and commercial intruder systems across the North West. Correct height, angle and pet-immune choices matter more than brand stickers; we get the placement right first time.
pir-sensor-installation.body: We fit PIRs to new systems and as upgrades on existing panels from major manufacturers. Dual-tech sensors combine PIR with microwave for tougher commercial environments, while curtain and volumetric patterns suit corridors, offices and open-plan homes. Installation includes neat mounting, correct zone programming, walk-testing and advice on furniture, heaters and blinds that cause false alarms. From Stockport we also attend reactive call-outs for failed or flashing PIRs.
pir-sensor-installation.meta_desc: PIR sensor installation & replacement. Pet-immune, dual-tech & corridor PIRs across Manchester, Stockport & the North West.
focus_points: Correct siting to cut false alarms and dead spots
Pet-immune and dual-tech options for homes and businesses
Compatible replacements for common panel brands
Walk-test and zone programming included
faq: Why does my PIR keep false alarming?
Common causes include heaters, direct sun, hanging objects, pests and incorrect sensitivity. We diagnose and re-site or replace the sensor rather than only silencing the panel.
Can PIRs work with pets?
Pet-immune models help with cats and small dogs when mounted and set correctly. Large dogs and free-roaming pets may still need careful design or alternative detection.
Do you install outdoor PIRs?
Yes, where suitable outdoor detectors are part of the design. Outdoor units need weather-rated devices and careful aiming to avoid neighbour movement and foliage.

police-response-alarm.name: Police Response Alarm
police-response-alarm.intro: Police response alarms must meet force and industry rules around Unique Reference Numbers (URNs), confirmed activations and professional monitoring. Icomply help North West businesses and high-risk sites design systems that support police response pathways — correct grade, confirmed alarms and ARC signalling — rather than relying on an unconfirmed bell-only system that will not attract attendance.
police-response-alarm.body: We survey risk, specify PD 6662-aligned equipment, and configure confirmation (for example sequential detection) so false call-outs are minimised. ARC partnership and URN application processes are explained step by step; response policies vary by force and change over time, so we design for current best practice and document the system thoroughly. Stockport engineers install, commission and maintain police-response-capable systems across Greater Manchester, Cheshire, Lancashire and Merseyside.
police-response-alarm.meta_desc: Police response alarm design & install. URN-ready, confirmed alarms & ARC monitoring across Greater Manchester & the North West.
focus_points: Design for confirmed activations and reduced false calls
PD 6662 grading and professional monitoring pathways
Support with ARC setup and URN-related documentation
Ongoing maintenance so response eligibility is not lost to neglect
faq: Does buying an alarm automatically get police response?
No. Response normally requires a monitored system meeting confirmation rules and a valid URN through an approved route. We design systems that support that process.
What is alarm confirmation?
Confirmation means more than one independent signal of an intrusion before police are requested, cutting false alarms. We programme detectors and zones to achieve this sensibly.
Can residential properties get police response?
It is less common than for commercial sites and depends on force policy and monitoring arrangements. We advise honestly after assessing your risk and location.
police-response-alarm.seo_keywords: police response alarm, URN alarm system, confirmed alarm installation, police response intruder alarm North West, ARC police response Manchester, PD 6662 police response

portable-appliance-testing.name: Portable Appliance Testing
portable-appliance-testing.intro: Portable appliance testing (PAT) checks plug-in electrical equipment used at work—and often in HMOs and managed housing—so damaged leads, failed insulation and incorrect fuses are caught before they cause shocks or fires. Icomply delivers practical PAT programmes for offices, workshops, hospitality and residential portfolios across the North West. From Stockport we label appliances, record results and focus effort on higher-risk kit rather than ritual testing of everything every month without reason.
portable-appliance-testing.body: Good PAT combines formal inspection with electrical tests suited to Class I and Class II equipment, plus clear fail tags when items must be removed from service. We help you set sensible retest intervals based on environment and usage, which keeps costs under control while meeting HSE expectations for maintenance of work equipment. Combined visits with fixed-wire EICRs or emergency lighting tests reduce contractor traffic on site. Digital asset lists make it easier for managers to track hundreds of appliances across multiple floors or addresses.
portable-appliance-testing.meta_desc: Portable appliance testing (PAT) for workplaces & portfolios across Greater Manchester & the North West. Clear labels & records from Icomply.
focus_points: On-site PAT inspection and testing with pass/fail labelling
Risk-based intervals for offices, workshops and HMOs
Asset lists and certificates for audit and insurance files
Combined compliance visits with other electrical services
faq: Is PAT testing a legal annual requirement?
The law requires work equipment to be maintained safely; PAT is a widely accepted method. Frequency should match risk—not an arbitrary one-size rule for every item.
Do you test IT equipment and extension leads?
Yes. Computers, monitors, leads and multi-way blocks are common inclusions; we can exclude user-owned devices if your policy requires.
What happens to failed appliances?
They are failed-labelled and should be removed from service until repaired or replaced. We can advise on common plug and lead repairs where appropriate.
portable-appliance-testing.seo_keywords: portable appliance testing, PAT testing, PAT test Manchester, workplace PAT Stockport, appliance testing North West, HMO PAT testing

power-flush-heating.name: Power Flush Heating
power-flush-heating.intro: A power flush removes sludge, rust and debris from central heating circuits so radiators heat evenly, boilers run more efficiently and component life improves. Icomply carry out power flush heating cleans with professional equipment, inhibitor dosing and system checks for homes and small commercial sites across Greater Manchester and the North West.
power-flush-heating.body: Symptoms such as cold radiator bottoms, noisy boilers, frequent pump failures or discoloured water often point to circulation problems. We isolate and flush circuits methodically, clean magnetite from low points, check strainer and pump condition, then refill with correct inhibitor strength. Where a plate heat exchanger or filter is advised we quote options clearly. Combine a power flush with annual boiler service for a stronger winter readiness package from our Stockport team.
power-flush-heating.meta_desc: Power flush heating cleans for even radiators & healthier boilers. Fixed quotes across Greater Manchester & the North West.
focus_points: Professional power flush of heating circuits and radiators
System refill with correct inhibitor dosing
Checks on pumps, strainers and magnetic filters
Optional link with boiler service or new boiler prep
faq: How long does a power flush take?
Most domestic systems take half a day to a day depending on radiator count, system condition and access. Larger or heavily fouled systems may need longer — we estimate after survey.
Will a power flush fix every heating problem?
It resolves many sludge-related issues but will not cure undersized boilers, faulty valves or major design faults. We diagnose honestly and recommend the right next step.
Is a power flush needed before a new boiler?
Manufacturers often require a clean system and inhibitor for warranty. We frequently power flush as part of boiler replacement packages.
power-flush-heating.seo_keywords: power flush heating, central heating power flush, power flush radiators, heating system flush North West, power flush Manchester, power flush Stockport, boiler sludge clean

power-surge-protection.name: Power Surge Protection
power-surge-protection.intro: Power surge protection shields sensitive equipment from spikes caused by switching, faults or nearby lightning activity. Icomply specifies and installs Type 1, Type 2 and combined SPD solutions for North West homes and businesses.
power-surge-protection.body: We assess supply arrangement, earthing and the value of connected loads before recommending devices at the origin or sub-distribution. Installation is coordinated with consumer unit upgrades where space is tight, and testing confirms correct connection. Advice covers when SPDs are strongly expected under modern BS 7671 practice.
power-surge-protection.meta_desc: Power surge protection (SPD) install for homes & businesses in Stockport, Greater Manchester & the North West. BS 7671 guidance from Icomply.
focus_points: Risk-based SPD selection for domestic and commercial supplies
Installation at main boards and key sub-distribution points
Coordination with consumer unit upgrades and earthing
Testing and certification of surge protection works
faq: Do all homes need surge protection?
BS 7671 places strong emphasis on SPDs in many installations. We assess your supply, location and equipment risk and explain whether devices are required or strongly recommended.
Will an SPD stop every power cut?
No — surge devices limit voltage spikes; they do not provide UPS-style backup during a total loss of supply.
Can SPDs be added to an existing consumer unit?
Often yes if space and cable routes allow. Otherwise we may recommend a board upgrade that includes integrated protection.
power-surge-protection.seo_keywords: power surge protection, SPD install Stockport, surge protection device Manchester, Type 2 SPD North West, lightning surge protection, Icomply electrical

preventive-electrical-maintenance.name: Preventive Electrical Maintenance
preventive-electrical-maintenance.intro: Preventive electrical maintenance catches loose terminations, failing protective devices and overloaded circuits before outages hit. Icomply builds practical PPM programmes for commercial and multi-residential sites around Stockport and the North West.
preventive-electrical-maintenance.body: Visits follow a defined checklist rather than a vague walk-round, with thermal attention on high-load boards where appropriate. Trends over successive visits help FM teams plan capital spend instead of reacting to every fault. Contracts are sized to the site — not a one-size-fits-all template.
preventive-electrical-maintenance.meta_desc: Preventive electrical maintenance & PPM contracts across Stockport, Manchester & the North West. Reduce downtime with Icomply.
focus_points: Scheduled PPM with documented checklists and defect logs
Focus on boards, protection devices and critical circuits
Trend reporting to support budget and capital planning
Reactive cover options alongside planned visits
faq: How often should preventive electrical maintenance run?
Many commercial sites choose six- or twelve-monthly visits, with higher risk environments needing more frequent checks. We recommend a frequency after survey.
Is preventive maintenance different from an EICR?
Yes. PPM is ongoing upkeep; an EICR is a formal condition assessment. Many clients use both as complementary tools.
Can you maintain mixed residential and commercial estates?
Yes — we support landlords and managing agents with blocks that combine retail, office and residential supplies.

proximity-card-reader.name: Proximity Card Reader
proximity-card-reader.intro: Proximity card readers are the workhorse of modern access control—fast, durable and simple for staff and residents to use. Icomply supplies and installs proximity card readers and compatible fobs/cards across the North West, wiring them to quality controllers and power supplies so reads stay reliable in busy doorways. We also replace failed or obsolete readers and re-enrol credentials with minimal downtime.
proximity-card-reader.body: Mullion, keypad-combo and vandal-resistant housings are matched to indoor, outdoor and car-park environments. Our Stockport team programmes site codes, manages lost-card revocation and can migrate from older 125 kHz systems to more secure formats where budgets allow. Ideal for offices, gyms, apartments and industrial staff doors. Fixed-price reader swaps or full multi-reader projects after survey. Pair with access panels, electric releases and door entry for complete entrance control.
proximity-card-reader.meta_desc: Proximity card reader install & replacement across Greater Manchester & the North West. Fobs, cards & controllers. Icomply, Stockport.
focus_points: Reader selection for indoor, outdoor and high-abuse doors
Install or like-for-like swap with neat cable management
Card/fob enrolment and lost-credential revocation
Controller pairing and optional migration to secure formats
faq: Why has our proximity reader stopped reading cards?
Causes include power faults, damaged coils, controller issues or cards from another site code. We diagnose before replacing parts unnecessarily.
Can phones replace proximity cards?
Many systems now support mobile credentials. We advise if your controller generation supports it or needs an upgrade path.
Are proximity cards secure enough for offices?
For most commercial doors, yes when paired with good admin processes. Higher-security areas may need smart cards, PIN+card or biometrics.
proximity-card-reader.seo_keywords: proximity card reader, prox reader install, access control fob, RFID door reader North West, card reader Manchester, proximity access Stockport, fob entry system

proximity-card-reader-installation.name: Proximity Card Reader Installation
proximity-card-reader-installation.intro: Proximity card reader installation gives staff and residents a fast, contactless way to enter authorised doors without shared keys. Icomply survey, supply, install and commission proximity readers as part of full access control systems for offices, blocks and multi-site estates, working to BS EN 50133 and fire-release best practice with programming and user notes included where agreed.
proximity-card-reader-installation.body: We position readers for usability and weather exposure, route cabling neatly, interface locks correctly and test free escape before handover. Stockport-based engineers serve Greater Manchester and the wider North West for single-door kits through to multi-door networked systems. If you already have controllers, we check compatibility before fitting new readers so cards, fobs and schedules continue to work as expected.
proximity-card-reader-installation.meta_desc: Proximity card reader installation across Greater Manchester & the North West. Icomply fit, program & commission readers with fixed-price quotes.
focus_points: Site survey for reader type, mounting and cable routes
Install of proximity readers with lock and egress interfaces
Credential setup, schedules and commissioning sheets
Replacement readers and upgrades on existing systems
faq: How long does proximity card reader installation take?
A single door can often be completed in a day once cable routes and power are agreed; multi-door projects are programmed after survey.
Can new readers use our existing cards?
If the credential technology matches the reader and controller, yes. We confirm formats during survey to avoid incompatible stock.
Do you install outdoor proximity readers?
Yes. We specify weather-rated readers and mounting for external doors, gates and car park access where suitable.

ptz-camera-installation.name: PTZ Camera Installation
ptz-camera-installation.intro: PTZ camera installation gives you pan, tilt and zoom control over large yards, car parks and open industrial floors where fixed cameras alone leave blind gaps. Icomply installs and commissions PTZ units across Greater Manchester and the North West—specifying mounts, brackets, power, network and presets so operators can patrol or jump to key views in seconds. We balance PTZ coverage with fixed cameras for continuous recording of critical doors and till points.
ptz-camera-installation.body: PTZ systems need sturdy poles or buildings, weather-rated housings and careful bandwidth planning on IP links. Our engineers programme home positions, patrol tours and privacy masks where required, and train nominated users on joystick or software control. Ideal for logistics depots, schools with large grounds and retail parks. Based in Stockport, we offer fixed-price quotes after survey including cabling routes and lightning/surge considerations for exposed mounts. Ask about hybrid fixed-plus-PTZ designs for best evidence quality.
ptz-camera-installation.meta_desc: PTZ camera installation across Greater Manchester & the North West. Pan-tilt-zoom CCTV, presets & training. Fixed quotes from Icomply.
focus_points: Survey for PTZ coverage, mounting height and power/network
Install of weather-rated PTZ with secure brackets and cabling
Preset tours, home positions and operator training
Hybrid design with fixed cameras for continuous critical views
faq: When should I choose PTZ over fixed CCTV?
Use PTZ for wide open areas where an operator needs to track activity. Keep fixed cameras on doors and high-risk points so evidence is continuous.
Can PTZ cameras record while moving?
Yes, but image quality during fast movement varies by model. We configure sensible speeds and still rely on fixed cameras for key chokepoints.
Do PTZ cameras need special maintenance?
Mechanical PTZs benefit from periodic checks of motors, seals and mounts—especially outdoors. We can include them on a planned service visit.
ptz-camera-installation.seo_keywords: PTZ camera installation, pan tilt zoom CCTV, PTZ CCTV North West, car park PTZ Manchester, PTZ install Stockport, outdoor PTZ camera, commercial PTZ system

pull-cord-nurse-call.name: Pull Cord Nurse Call
pull-cord-nurse-call.intro: Want pull cord nurse call points that residents can reach in bathrooms and bedrooms — installed, repaired and regularly tested? Icomply fit and service pull cord nurse call devices for care homes and healthcare sites across Greater Manchester. Our Stockport team ensures cords, switches and indicators work reliably with fixed-price maintenance options.
pull-cord-nurse-call.body: Pull cords are simple but safety-critical: a broken cord, sticky switch or poorly sited unit can leave a resident unable to call for help. We install cords at correct heights, with free travel and visible over-door or panel indication, and we replace frayed or yellowed cords before they fail. Bathroom installs consider wet environments and reset locations per policy. During PPM we operate every cord and log results. Reactive repairs restore isolated failures without waiting for the next service visit. Cover includes Cheshire, Lancashire, Merseyside and Cumbria.
pull-cord-nurse-call.meta_desc: Pull cord nurse call install, repair & testing for care homes. Icomply engineers — Stockport & North West.
focus_points: Correct siting and free-travel pull cord installs
Bathroom and en-suite wet-area considerations
Frayed cord replacement before failure
Full functional testing within PPM visits
faq: How long should a pull cord be?
Long enough to reach from the floor in bathrooms per good practice and your risk assessment — we set out during install.
Why does my cord not reset?
Reset may be local or at panel depending on design; mechanical switch faults are also common and repairable.
Can cords be disabled for infection control?
Temporary measures must never leave residents without a call method — discuss alternatives with us and your manager.

Can I add devices to my current Pyronix system?

rcbo-consumer-unit.name: RCBO Consumer Unit
rcbo-consumer-unit.intro: An RCBO consumer unit gives each circuit combined overcurrent and residual current protection, limiting the inconvenience of a single RCD taking out half the house when one appliance faults. Icomply supplies and installs RCBO consumer units for homes, HMOs and light commercial sites across Greater Manchester and the North West. From Stockport we design board layouts, label every way clearly, and certificate the upgrade to BS 7671.
rcbo-consumer-unit.body: All-RCBO boards are popular for larger properties and rentals where nuisance tripping causes tenant complaints. We select RCBO ratings to match cable sizes and loads, consider surge protection devices where appropriate, and check main earth and bonding before energising. Existing circuits are dressed neatly into the new enclosure with spare ways for future EV or kitchen circuits where space allows. After testing you will know exactly which RCBO protects which room—useful for any future fault finding.
rcbo-consumer-unit.meta_desc: RCBO consumer unit install across Greater Manchester & the North West. All-RCBO boards, testing & certificates from Icomply Stockport.
focus_points: All-RCBO consumer unit design and installation
Reduced nuisance tripping compared with dual-RCD boards
Surge protection options and spare ways for expansion
Full BS 7671 testing and Electrical Installation Certificate
faq: Is an RCBO board better than dual RCD?
RCBOs isolate faults to a single circuit, which many households prefer. Dual-RCD boards can still be compliant; we explain trade-offs on survey.
Are RCBO consumer units more expensive?
Device cost is higher than shared RCDs, but many clients value the resilience—especially in rentals and larger homes.
Can RCBOs be added to my existing board?
Sometimes individual ways can be upgraded if the board accepts RCBOs; often a full board replacement is cleaner and more reliable.

remote-cctv-access.name: Remote CCTV Access
remote-cctv-access.intro: Remote CCTV access lets owners and managers view live and recorded video from anywhere with a secure connection—vital for multi-site estates, out-of-hours response and travelling directors. Icomply configures remote CCTV access across the North West with encrypted apps or browsers, strong passwords, two-factor options where supported, and network settings that do not expose your whole LAN. Fixed-price setup after we understand your recorder model and broadband.
remote-cctv-access.body: Poor remote setups—default ports, shared passwords, UPnP free-for-alls—are a common security risk. Our Stockport engineers harden access, create named users and train you on mobile and desktop viewing. We also verify that remote sessions do not stop local recording. Compatible with IP CCTV systems, commercial NVRs and many hybrid recorders. Covering Greater Manchester and surrounding counties, we can attend site or guide IT contacts through firewall rules when preferred.
remote-cctv-access.meta_desc: Secure remote CCTV access setup across Greater Manchester & the North West. Apps, users & encryption. Fixed quotes from Icomply.
focus_points: Encrypted remote viewing via app or browser
Named user accounts instead of shared passwords
Router/firewall configuration that protects the LAN
Training for managers on live view, search and clips
faq: Is remote CCTV access safe?
When configured with strong authentication, updates and network segmentation, yes. We avoid insecure shortcuts and document the setup for your IT team.
Can I view CCTV without a static IP address?
Often yes using manufacturer cloud services, dynamic DNS or VPN approaches. We recommend the option that fits your recorder and risk appetite.
Why is my remote CCTV video lagging?
Usually bandwidth, stream settings or congested Wi-Fi. We optimise sub-streams for mobile and keep full quality for local recording.

residential-care-call-system.intro: Planning a residential care call system for a new build, refurbishment or compliance refresh? Icomply deliver nurse call design, installation and ongoing maintenance for residential care settings throughout Greater Manchester and the North West. Aligned with HTM 08-03 thinking and day-to-day care practice, we help providers give residents a simple way to request help while giving staff clear visual and audible alerts from a central base or mobile devices.
residential-care-call-system.body: Residential care call systems should feel unobtrusive yet highly reliable. We survey room layouts, corridor sightlines and staff circulation routes, then propose call points, pull cords, overdoor lights and optional door monitoring or staff-attack devices. Installation includes neat containment, labelled circuits, power and battery standby, and full functional testing before handover. Service contracts cover periodic checks, consumables such as pear leads and batteries, and priority call-outs when a wing loses indication. Operators receive PPM records suitable for internal audits and external scrutiny. From our Stockport base we support single homes and regional portfolios with fixed-price quotes, manufacturer guidance and coordinated multi-site scheduling.

Requirements depend on your registration, fire risk assessment and care model. Most residential care providers install nurse call as a core safety and quality measure; we design to recognised healthcare call guidance and your operational needs.

Yes. We position call points and leads for reach from bed and chair, specify large-button or pull-cord options where helpful, and check usability during commissioning.

Share floor plans or arrange a free survey. We confirm device counts, cabling condition and preferred brands, then issue a fixed-price quote after scope agreement.

roof-aov.name: Roof AOV
roof-aov.intro: Roof AOVs are smoke vents mounted at roof level to exhaust smoke from stairs, atria or shafts using natural buoyancy. Icomply install, weatherproof, commission and maintain roof AOVs across the North West — including safe access planning, actuator service and control-panel integration for residential and commercial buildings from our Stockport base.
roof-aov.body: Roof works need careful sequencing for weather, fall protection and resident safety. We fit glazed or insulated roof vents, connect drives to the AOV panel, test full opening against design free area and ensure drainage and flashings remain sound. Maintenance includes seal checks, debris clearance around kerbs and motor health. Failed roof vents that leak or seize are repaired or replaced with fixed quotes after inspection, often coordinated with other roof-level life-safety equipment.
roof-aov.meta_desc: Roof AOV install, weatherproofing, service & repair. Natural smoke vents at roof level. Stockport, Manchester & North West.
focus_points: Roof AOV supply and weatherproof installation
Safe access methods for height work
Actuator and free-area commissioning
Roof-level maintenance and leak-related remedials
faq: How do roof AOVs differ from window AOVs?
Mounting, weathering and often free-area geometry differ; both serve smoke control but need location-specific products and access plans.
Will a roof AOV make the building leak?
Correct kerb, flashing and seal detailing prevent leaks — poor install is the usual cause of water ingress, which we avoid and can remediate.
How often should roof AOVs be serviced?
At least as often as the rest of the smoke-control system, with extra attention after severe weather if damage is suspected.
roof-aov.seo_keywords: roof AOV, roof smoke vent, roof automatic opening vent, roof AOV install, roof AOV Manchester, roof AOV Stockport, natural roof smoke vent, North West roof AOV
